Plan breakout game implementation

```text
The LVGL version is actually **not** 8.2.0 - looking at the APIs I found (like `lv_disp_load_scr`, the obj tree structure, etc.), this appears to be a development branch between 8.2 and 9.0. The lv_conf.h comment says "v9.0.0-dev" but the `lvgl.h` declares `LVGL_VERSION_MAJOR 8` and `LVGL_VERSION_MINOR 2`. The API signatures match LVGL 8.x conventions (separate style gen functions with selectors, `lv_obj_create(parent)`, etc.). I'll design for the actual API present in the codebase.

Now I have enough information to create a comprehensive implementation plan.

---

## Breakout Game Implementation Plan for LVGL on SAME51

### 1. File Structure and Module Responsibilities

```
src/
  app.c              (MODIFIED) - Remove demo call, init splash screen instead
  app.h              (UNCHANGED)
  game/
    game_common.h    - Shared types, constants, screen dimensions
    splash_screen.c  - Splash screen UI creation and event handling
    splash_screen.h  - Public interface: splash_screen_create(), splash_screen_destroy()
    breakout.c       - Game logic, timer callback, collision, rendering
    breakout.h       - Public interface: breakout_create(), breakout_destroy()
```

**Module responsibilities:**

- **game_common.h**: Central definitions -- display dimensions (480x320), game area bounds, shared enums for game states, forward declarations.
- **splash_screen.c/.h**: Creates an LVGL screen with a title label ("BREAKOUT") and a "Tap to Start" label. Registers a click event on the screen that transitions to the game.
- **breakout.c/.h**: Contains all game logic -- creates game objects on a new screen, runs the game loop timer, handles collisions, manages score/lives, transitions to game-over state and back to splash.
- **app.c**: Modified to `#include "game/splash_screen.h"` and replace the demo widget call with `splash_screen_create()`.

### 2. Data Structures for Game State

```c
/* game_common.h */
#define SCREEN_WIDTH    480
#define SCREEN_HEIGHT   320

#define BRICK_COLS      10
#define BRICK_ROWS      5
#define BRICK_COUNT     (BRICK_COLS * BRICK_ROWS)  /* 50 bricks */

#define PADDLE_WIDTH    80
#define PADDLE_HEIGHT   12
#define PADDLE_Y        (SCREEN_HEIGHT - 30)

#define BALL_SIZE       10

#define GAME_TIMER_MS   20   /* 50 Hz physics, runs within 33ms LVGL refresh */

#define MAX_LIVES       3

typedef enum {
    GAME_STATE_IDLE,       /* Ball stuck to paddle before launch */
    GAME_STATE_PLAYING,    /* Ball in motion */
    GAME_STATE_GAME_OVER,  /* All lives lost */
    GAME_STATE_WIN         /* All bricks destroyed */
} game_state_t;
```

```c
/* breakout.c - internal state structure */
typedef struct {
    /* LVGL objects (pre-allocated, never destroyed during gameplay) */
    lv_obj_t *screen;
    lv_obj_t *paddle;
    lv_obj_t *ball;
    lv_obj_t *bricks[BRICK_COUNT];
    lv_obj_t *score_label;
    lv_obj_t *lives_label;
    lv_obj_t *message_label;   /* "GAME OVER" / "YOU WIN" / "Tap to launch" */
    
    /* Game timer */
    lv_timer_t *game_timer;
    
    /* Physics state (fixed-point: multiply by 16 for sub-pixel precision) */
    int16_t ball_x;           /* Ball center X (actual pixels) */
    int16_t ball_y;           /* Ball center Y (actual pixels) */
    int16_t ball_vx;          /* Ball velocity X (pixels per tick, scaled x16) */
    int16_t ball_vy;          /* Ball velocity Y (pixels per tick, scaled x16) */
    int16_t ball_x_frac;      /* Fractional X accumulator */
    int16_t ball_y_frac;      /* Fractional Y accumulator */
    
    /* Paddle state */
    int16_t paddle_x;         /* Paddle left edge X */
    
    /* Game state */
    game_state_t state;
    uint8_t lives;
    uint16_t score;
    uint8_t bricks_remaining;
    bool brick_alive[BRICK_COUNT];  /* Track which bricks are visible */
} breakout_state_t;

static breakout_state_t g_game;  /* Single static instance - no malloc */
```

**Why fixed-point velocity:** The ball needs smooth diagonal motion at sub-pixel granularity. Using `int16_t` with a 4-bit fractional part (x16 scale) gives adequate precision without requiring floating point (the Cortex-M4F has FPU, but integer is simpler and deterministic for game physics).

### 3. Game Loop Logic (Timer Callback Flow)

```c
static void game_timer_cb(lv_timer_t *timer) {
    breakout_state_t *g = &g_game;
    
    if (g->state != GAME_STATE_PLAYING) return;
    
    /* 1. Read touch input and update paddle position */
    update_paddle(g);
    
    /* 2. Move ball using fixed-point accumulation */
    g->ball_x_frac += g->ball_vx;
    g->ball_y_frac += g->ball_vy;
    int16_t dx = g->ball_x_frac / 16;
    int16_t dy = g->ball_y_frac / 16;
    g->ball_x_frac -= dx * 16;
    g->ball_y_frac -= dy * 16;
    g->ball_x += dx;
    g->ball_y += dy;
    
    /* 3. Collision detection (order matters) */
    check_wall_collision(g);
    check_paddle_collision(g);
    check_brick_collision(g);
    check_ball_lost(g);
    
    /* 4. Update LVGL object positions */
    lv_obj_set_pos(g->ball, g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE/2, g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE/2);
    lv_obj_set_pos(g->paddle, g->paddle_x, PADDLE_Y);
    
    /* 5. Check win condition */
    if (g->bricks_remaining == 0) {
        g->state = GAME_STATE_WIN;
        show_message(g, "YOU WIN!");
    }
}
```

**Timer creation** (in `breakout_create()`):
```c
g_game.game_timer = lv_timer_create(game_timer_cb, GAME_TIMER_MS, NULL);
```

### 4. Collision Detection Approach

**Ball vs Walls (left, right, top):**
```c
static void check_wall_collision(breakout_state_t *g) {
    /* Left wall */
    if (g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE/2 <= 0) {
        g->ball_x = BALL_SIZE/2;
        g->ball_vx = -g->ball_vx;
    }
    /* Right wall */
    if (g->ball_x + BALL_SIZE/2 >= SCREEN_WIDTH) {
        g->ball_x = SCREEN_WIDTH - BALL_SIZE/2;
        g->ball_vx = -g->ball_vx;
    }
    /* Top wall */
    if (g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE/2 <= 0) {
        g->ball_y = BALL_SIZE/2;
        g->ball_vy = -g->ball_vy;
    }
}
```

**Ball vs Paddle:**
```c
static void check_paddle_collision(breakout_state_t *g) {
    /* Only check when ball is moving downward */
    if (g->ball_vy <= 0) return;
    
    int16_t ball_bottom = g->ball_y + BALL_SIZE/2;
    int16_t ball_left = g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE/2;
    int16_t ball_right = g->ball_x + BALL_SIZE/2;
    
    /* AABB overlap test */
    if (ball_bottom >= PADDLE_Y &&
        ball_bottom <= PADDLE_Y + PADDLE_HEIGHT &&
        ball_right >= g->paddle_x &&
        ball_left <= g->paddle_x + PADDLE_WIDTH) {
        
        /* Reflect vertically */
        g->ball_vy = -g->ball_vy;
        g->ball_y = PADDLE_Y - BALL_SIZE/2;
        
        /* Adjust horizontal velocity based on hit position for angle control */
        int16_t hit_pos = g->ball_x - (g->paddle_x + PADDLE_WIDTH/2);
        /* Map hit_pos from [-PADDLE_WIDTH/2, +PADDLE_WIDTH/2] to vx adjustment */
        g->ball_vx = (hit_pos * 48) / (PADDLE_WIDTH/2);  /* Max vx = +/-48 (3 px/tick) */
    }
}
```

**Ball vs Bricks:**
```c
static void check_brick_collision(breakout_state_t *g) {
    for (int i = 0; i < BRICK_COUNT; i++) {
        if (!g->brick_alive[i]) continue;
        
        /* Calculate brick position from index */
        int col = i % BRICK_COLS;
        int row = i / BRICK_COLS;
        int16_t bx = BRICK_MARGIN_LEFT + col * (BRICK_WIDTH + BRICK_GAP_H);
        int16_t by = BRICK_MARGIN_TOP + row * (BRICK_HEIGHT + BRICK_GAP_V);
        
        /* AABB test: ball circle approximated as square */
        int16_t ball_left = g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE/2;
        int16_t ball_right = g->ball_x + BALL_SIZE/2;
        int16_t ball_top = g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE/2;
        int16_t ball_bottom = g->ball_y + BALL_SIZE/2;
        
        if (ball_right >= bx && ball_left <= bx + BRICK_WIDTH &&
            ball_bottom >= by && ball_top <= by + BRICK_HEIGHT) {
            
            /* Destroy brick: hide LVGL object (do NOT delete) */
            g->brick_alive[i] = false;
            lv_obj_add_flag(g->bricks[i], LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
            g->bricks_remaining--;
            g->score += 10;
            update_score_display(g);
            
            /* Determine reflection axis based on penetration depth */
            int16_t overlap_left = ball_right - bx;
            int16_t overlap_right = (bx + BRICK_WIDTH) - ball_left;
            int16_t overlap_top = ball_bottom - by;
            int16_t overlap_bottom = (by + BRICK_HEIGHT) - ball_top;
            int16_t min_overlap_x = (overlap_left < overlap_right) ? overlap_left : overlap_right;
            int16_t min_overlap_y = (overlap_top < overlap_bottom) ? overlap_top : overlap_bottom;
            
            if (min_overlap_x < min_overlap_y) {
                g->ball_vx = -g->ball_vx;
            } else {
                g->ball_vy = -g->ball_vy;
            }
            
            break;  /* Only destroy one brick per tick to avoid tunneling artifacts */
        }
    }
}
```

**Ball lost (falls below paddle):**
```c
static void check_ball_lost(breakout_state_t *g) {
    if (g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE/2 > SCREEN_HEIGHT) {
        g->lives--;
        update_lives_display(g);
        if (g->lives == 0) {
            g->state = GAME_STATE_GAME_OVER;
            show_message(g, "GAME OVER");
        } else {
            /* Reset ball to paddle, wait for tap */
            g->state = GAME_STATE_IDLE;
            reset_ball_to_paddle(g);
            show_message(g, "Tap to launch");
        }
    }
}
```

### 5. Screen Management (Splash -> Game -> Game Over -> Splash)

**Screen transition architecture:**

Each module owns its own `lv_obj_t *screen` created with `lv_obj_create(NULL)` (parentless = screen object). Transitions use `lv_scr_load()` for immediate switch (no animation to save memory).

```
[Splash Screen] --tap--> [Breakout Screen (IDLE state)]
                              |
                              v
                         [PLAYING state]
                              |
                              v (lives=0)
                         [GAME_OVER overlay on same screen]
                              |
                              v (tap)
                         [Splash Screen]  (or restart)
```

**Implementation flow:**

1. `splash_screen_create()`: Creates screen, adds title + instruction labels, registers `LV_EVENT_CLICKED` on the screen object.
2. On click: calls `breakout_create()` which creates game screen and loads it via `lv_scr_load()`. The splash screen is deleted (`lv_obj_del(splash_scr)`) after load.
3. Game over/win: Displays message label overlay on the game screen. Registers click event to either restart (recreate bricks, reset state) or go to splash.
4. Going back to splash: `breakout_destroy()` deletes game timer and game screen, then `splash_screen_create()` creates fresh splash.

**Key API calls:**
```c
/* Create a new screen (not child of anything) */
lv_obj_t *scr = lv_obj_create(NULL);

/* Switch to it */
lv_scr_load(scr);

/* Delete old screen after switching */
lv_obj_del(old_scr);
```

### 6. Touch Input Mapping for Paddle Control

The touch input is already available via the LVGL input device system (touchX, touchY globals are fed to LVGL's indev). However, for the game loop we need direct access without going through LVGL events. The approach:

**Option A (preferred): Read touch globals directly.**

Since `touchX`, `touchY`, `touchDown` are file-scope statics in `app.c`, we need to expose them. Add to `app.h`:
```c
extern uint32_t g_touchX;
extern uint32_t g_touchY;  
extern bool g_touchDown;
```

Then in `app.c` rename the statics to these globals (or add accessor functions).

**Paddle update logic:**
```c
static void update_paddle(breakout_state_t *g) {
    extern uint32_t g_touchX;
    extern bool g_touchDown;
    
    if (g_touchDown) {
        /* Direct mapping: paddle center follows touch X */
        int16_t target_x = (int16_t)g_touchX - PADDLE_WIDTH / 2;
        
        /* Clamp to screen bounds */
        if (target_x < 0) target_x = 0;
        if (target_x > SCREEN_WIDTH - PADDLE_WIDTH) target_x = SCREEN_WIDTH - PADDLE_WIDTH;
        
        g->paddle_x = target_x;
    }
}
```

**Alternative (Option B): Use `lv_indev_get_point()`** to read the current touch position through LVGL's indev API. This is cleaner but adds a function call:
```c
lv_indev_t *indev = lv_indev_get_act();  /* or store reference */
lv_point_t p;
lv_indev_get_point(indev, &p);
```

I recommend Option A for lowest latency and simplest code, since the globals already exist.

### 7. Performance Considerations for the MCU

**Critical performance rules:**

1. **Pre-allocate all LVGL objects at game start.** Never create or delete objects during gameplay. For bricks, create all 50 at init and hide/show them with `LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN`.

2. **Minimize style operations.** Set all styles once during object creation. During gameplay, only call `lv_obj_set_pos()` for the ball and paddle (2 objects per frame). Hidden bricks are skipped by LVGL's renderer.

3. **Disable scrolling on game screen:**
   ```c
   lv_obj_clear_flag(screen, LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE);
   ```

4. **Use `lv_obj_set_pos()` not `lv_obj_set_x()` + `lv_obj_set_y()` separately.** One call triggers one layout update instead of two.

5. **Game timer at 20ms** (50 Hz physics) runs inside the 33ms LVGL refresh period. LVGL's dirty-area tracking means only the ball's old and new positions plus the paddle get redrawn. Bricks are static once rendered.

6. **Ball size = 10x10 pixels.** The dirty area per frame is approximately (10+max_speed*2) x (10+max_speed*2) pixels for the ball, plus paddle width when it moves. Well within the 480x200 display buffer capacity.

7. **No floating point in game loop.** All physics use integer arithmetic with fixed-point scaling.

8. **Score label: use `lv_label_set_text_fmt()`** instead of `sprintf` + `lv_label_set_text()` to avoid an extra buffer.

9. **Memory budget estimate:**
   - 50 brick objects: ~50 * 200 bytes = ~10 KB (lv_obj_t size in LVGL 8.x is ~150-200 bytes)
   - Ball, paddle, labels, screens: ~5 objects * 200 = ~1 KB
   - Game state struct: ~120 bytes
   - Total LVGL heap usage for game: ~11-12 KB (fits well within 48 KB budget)

10. **Disable `LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE` on ball, paddle, and bricks** to prevent LVGL from doing hit-testing on these objects:
    ```c
    lv_obj_clear_flag(obj, LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
    ```

### 8. Brick Layout Calculation for 480x320 Display

**Layout parameters:**

```c
#define BRICK_COLS       10
#define BRICK_ROWS       5
#define BRICK_WIDTH      40    /* pixels */
#define BRICK_HEIGHT     14    /* pixels */
#define BRICK_GAP_H      4    /* horizontal gap between bricks */
#define BRICK_GAP_V      4    /* vertical gap between bricks */
#define BRICK_MARGIN_TOP  30  /* top margin (space for score/lives labels) */
#define BRICK_MARGIN_LEFT 12  /* left margin for centering */
```

**Verification:**
- Total brick+gap width: 10 bricks * 40px + 9 gaps * 4px = 400 + 36 = 436 px
- Left margin: (480 - 436) / 2 = 22 px (or use 12px if you want slightly left-aligned with 32px right margin -- either works)
- Actually with symmetric centering: `BRICK_MARGIN_LEFT = (480 - (BRICK_COLS * BRICK_WIDTH + (BRICK_COLS-1) * BRICK_GAP_H)) / 2 = (480 - 436) / 2 = 22`
- Total brick+gap height: 5 rows * 14px + 4 gaps * 4px = 70 + 16 = 86 px
- Brick area: from y=30 to y=116
- Play area: y=116 to y=320 (204 px for ball to travel)
- Paddle at y=290 (PADDLE_Y = 290)

**Brick colors by row** (for visual appeal, stored as constant array):
```c
static const lv_color_t brick_colors[BRICK_ROWS] = {
    LV_COLOR_MAKE(0xFF, 0x00, 0x00),  /* Row 0: Red */
    LV_COLOR_MAKE(0xFF, 0xA5, 0x00),  /* Row 1: Orange */
    LV_COLOR_MAKE(0xFF, 0xFF, 0x00),  /* Row 2: Yellow */
    LV_COLOR_MAKE(0x00, 0xFF, 0x00),  /* Row 3: Green */
    LV_COLOR_MAKE(0x00, 0xBF, 0xFF),  /* Row 4: Cyan */
};
```

**Brick creation loop:**
```c
for (int row = 0; row < BRICK_ROWS; row++) {
    for (int col = 0; col < BRICK_COLS; col++) {
        int idx = row * BRICK_COLS + col;
        int16_t x = BRICK_MARGIN_LEFT + col * (BRICK_WIDTH + BRICK_GAP_H);
        int16_t y = BRICK_MARGIN_TOP + row * (BRICK_HEIGHT + BRICK_GAP_V);
        
        g->bricks[idx] = lv_obj_create(g->screen);
        lv_obj_set_size(g->bricks[idx], BRICK_WIDTH, BRICK_HEIGHT);
        lv_obj_set_pos(g->bricks[idx], x, y);
        lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(g->bricks[idx], brick_colors[row], LV_PART_MAIN);
        lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(g->bricks[idx], LV_OPA_COVER, LV_PART_MAIN);
        lv_obj_set_style_radius(g->bricks[idx], 2, LV_PART_MAIN);
        lv_obj_set_style_border_width(g->bricks[idx], 0, LV_PART_MAIN);
        lv_obj_clear_flag(g->bricks[idx], LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE | LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
        
        g->brick_alive[idx] = true;
    }
}
```

### 9. Complete Implementation Sequence

**Step 1: Create `src/game/game_common.h`**
- All #defines for dimensions, speeds, layout
- Include guards, stdint/stdbool includes

**Step 2: Create `src/game/splash_screen.h` and `src/game/splash_screen.c`**
- `void splash_screen_create(void)` -- creates and loads splash screen
- Internal: screen click handler calls `breakout_create()`
- Uses Montserrat 16 for title, Montserrat 14 for instruction

**Step 3: Create `src/game/breakout.h` and `src/game/breakout.c`**
- `void breakout_create(void)` -- creates game screen, all objects, timer; loads screen
- `void breakout_destroy(void)` -- deletes timer, deletes screen
- Internal: `game_timer_cb()`, collision functions, state management
- On game-over tap: call `breakout_destroy()` then `splash_screen_create()`

**Step 4: Modify `src/app.c`**
- Add `#include "game/splash_screen.h"`
- Change touch variables from `static` to non-static (add extern declarations to a header or game_common.h)
- Replace the `#if LV_USE_DEMO_WIDGETS ... #endif` block (lines 211-219) with `splash_screen_create();`

**Step 5: Update build system**
- Add `src/game/splash_screen.c` and `src/game/breakout.c` to the MPLAB X project's `configurations.xml` (source files section)
- Add `src/game/` to the include path in the project settings

**Step 6: Optionally disable demo widgets**
- In `lv_conf.h`, set `#define LV_USE_DEMO_WIDGETS 0` to save flash/RAM (the demo widget code won't be linked)

### 10. Ball Launch and Reset Mechanics

```c
static void reset_ball_to_paddle(breakout_state_t *g) {
    g->ball_x = g->paddle_x + PADDLE_WIDTH / 2;
    g->ball_y = PADDLE_Y - BALL_SIZE / 2 - 1;
    g->ball_vx = 0;
    g->ball_vy = 0;
    g->ball_x_frac = 0;
    g->ball_y_frac = 0;
    lv_obj_set_pos(g->ball, g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE/2, g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE/2);
}

static void launch_ball(breakout_state_t *g) {
    /* Launch upward with slight random angle */
    g->ball_vx = 16;    /* 1 pixel per tick in x16 fixed-point */
    g->ball_vy = -48;   /* 3 pixels per tick upward */
    g->state = GAME_STATE_PLAYING;
    lv_obj_add_flag(g->message_label, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
}
```

The launch is triggered by a tap event (screen click) when `state == GAME_STATE_IDLE`.

### 11. Screen Event Handling

```c
static void game_screen_click_cb(lv_event_t *e) {
    breakout_state_t *g = &g_game;
    
    switch (g->state) {
        case GAME_STATE_IDLE:
            launch_ball(g);
            break;
        case GAME_STATE_GAME_OVER:
        case GAME_STATE_WIN:
            /* Return to splash */
            breakout_destroy();
            splash_screen_create();
            break;
        default:
            break;
    }
}
```

Register on the game screen:
```c
lv_obj_add_event_cb(g->screen, game_screen_click_cb, LV_EVENT_CLICKED, NULL);
lv_obj_add_flag(g->screen, LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
```

### 12. Potential Challenges and Mitigations

| Challenge | Mitigation |
|-----------|-----------|
| Ball tunneling through thin bricks at high speed | Cap ball speed; bricks are 14px tall vs max 3px/tick movement = safe |
| LVGL heap exhaustion with 50+ objects | Budget verified: ~12KB of 48KB. Disable unused widgets in lv_conf.h |
| Touch latency causing jerky paddle | Direct read of `touchX` in timer callback, no smoothing/filtering needed |
| Screen tearing | LVGL's dirty-area approach with 480x200 buffer handles partial updates smoothly |
| `lv_obj_set_pos()` cost | Only 2 calls per frame (ball + paddle). Bricks are static. |
| Collision with multiple bricks simultaneously | Process only first hit per frame to keep physics stable |

---

- `c:\microchip\mchp_fw\mchpgfx\mgsh_lvgl\third_party\lvgl\src\core\lv_obj_pos.h`

**Architecture requirements:**
1. Remove the demo widget call and replace with custom game screens
2. Create a splash screen that prompts user to tap to start the game
3. Create the Breakout game itself
4. Use LVGL objects (rectangles, labels) rather than canvas for performance (LVGL handles dirty-area tracking)
5. Use `lv_timer_create()` for the game loop (16-33ms period)
6. Touch X position controls the paddle (direct mapping, paddle follows finger horizontally)

**Game design for Breakout:**
- Paddle: wide rectangle at bottom, controlled by touch X
- Ball: small square/circle that bounces off walls, paddle, and bricks
- Bricks: grid of colored rectangles at the top
- Score display: label in corner
- Lives: 3 lives, game over when all lost
- Win condition: all bricks destroyed

- `lv_obj_set_pos()`, `lv_obj_set_size()` for positioning
- `lv_obj_set_style_bg_color()` for colors

- `lv_label_create()`, `lv_label_set_text()` for score/lives
- `lv_timer_create(callback, period, user_data)` for game loop
- `lv_scr_load()` or `lv_scr_load_anim()` for screen transitions
- `lv_obj_get_x()`, `lv_obj_get_y()` for position reads

**File organization plan:**
- src/game/splash_screen.c/.h — splash screen UI
- src/game/breakout.c/.h — game logic and rendering
- src/app.c — modified to call splash screen instead of demos

108	
109	/*Enable the built in mask engine.

115	
116	    /*Enable complex draw engine.
117	     *Required to draw shadow, gradient, rounded corners, circles, arc, skew lines, image transformations or any masks*/
118	    #define LV_DRAW_SW_COMPLEX 1
119	
120	    /* If a widget has `style_opa < 255` (not `bg_opa`, `text_opa` etc) or not NORMAL blend mode
121	     * it is buffered into a "simple" layer before rendering. The widget can be buffered in smaller chunks.

123	     * and can't be drawn in chunks. */
124	
125	    /*The target buffer size for simple layer chunks.*/
126	    #define LV_DRAW_SW_LAYER_SIMPLE_BUF_SIZE          (24 * 1024)   /*[bytes]*/

128	    /*Used if `LV_DRAW_SW_LAYER_SIMPLE_BUF_SIZE` couldn't be allocated.*/
129	    #define LV_DRAW_SW_LAYER_SIMPLE_FALLBACK_BUF_SIZE (3 * 1024)    /*[bytes]*/
130	
131	    /*Allow buffering some shadow calculation.
132	    *LV_DRAW_SW_SHADOW_CACHE_SIZE is the max. shadow size to buffer, where shadow size is `shadow_width + radius`

134	    #define LV_DRAW_SW_SHADOW_CACHE_SIZE 0
135	
136	    /* Set number of maximally cached circle data.
137	    * The circumference of 1/4 circle are saved for anti-aliasing
138	    * radius * 4 bytes are used per circle (the most often used radiuses are saved)
139	    * 0: to disable caching */
140	    #define LV_DRAW_SW_CIRCLE_CACHE_SIZE 4
141	
142	    /*Default gradient buffer size.
143	     *When LVGL calculates the gradient "maps" it can save them into a cache to avoid calculating them again.
144	     *LV_DRAW_SW_GRADIENT_CACHE_DEF_SIZE sets the size of this cache in bytes.
145	     *If the cache is too small the map will be allocated only while it's required for the drawing.
146	     *0 mean no caching.*/
147	    #define LV_DRAW_SW_GRADIENT_CACHE_DEF_SIZE 0
148	
149	    /*Allow dithering the gradients (to achieve visual smooth color gradients on limited color depth display)
150	     *LV_DRAW_SW_GRADIENT_DITHER implies allocating one or two more lines of the object's rendering surface
151	     *The increase in memory consumption is (32 bits * object width) plus 24 bits * object width if using error diffusion */
152	    #define LV_DRAW_SW_GRADIENT_DITHER 0
153	    #if LV_DRAW_SW_GRADIENT_DITHER
154	        /*Add support for error diffusion dithering.
155	         *Error diffusion dithering gets a much better visual result, but implies more CPU consumption and memory when drawing.
156	         *The increase in memory consumption is (24 bits * object's width)*/
157	        #define LV_DRAW_SW_GRADIENT_DITHER_ERROR_DIFFUSION 0
158	    #endif
159	
160	    /*Enable subpixel rendering*/
161	    #define LV_DRAW_SW_FONT_SUBPX 0
162	    #if LV_DRAW_SW_FONT_SUBPX
163	        /*Set the pixel order of the display. Physical order of RGB channels. Doesn't matter with "normal" fonts.*/
164	        #define LV_DRAW_SW_FONT_SUBPX_BGR 0  /*0: RGB; 1:BGR order*/
165	    #endif
166	#endif
167	
168	/*Use SDL renderer API*/
169	#define LV_USE_DRAW_SDL 0
170	#if LV_USE_DRAW_SDL
171	    #define LV_DRAW_SDL_INCLUDE_PATH <SDL2/SDL.h>
172	    /*Texture cache size, 8MB by default*/
173	    #define LV_DRAW_SDL_LRU_SIZE (1024 * 1024 * 8)
174	    /*Custom blend mode for mask drawing, disable if you need to link with older SDL2 lib*/
175	    #define LV_DRAW_SDL_CUSTOM_BLEND_MODE (SDL_VERSION_ATLEAST(2, 0, 6))
176	#endif
177	
178	/*=====================
179	 * GPU CONFIGURATION
180	 *=====================*/
181	
182	/*Use Arm's 2D acceleration library Arm-2D */
183	#define LV_USE_GPU_ARM2D 0
184	
185	/*Use STM32's DMA2D (aka Chrom Art) GPU*/
186	#define LV_USE_GPU_STM32_DMA2D 0
187	#if LV_USE_GPU_STM32_DMA2D
188	    /*Must be defined to include path of CMSIS header of target processor
189	    e.g. "stm32f769xx.h" or "stm32f429xx.h"*/
190	    #define LV_GPU_DMA2D_CMSIS_INCLUDE
191	#endif
192	
193	/*Use GD32 IPA GPU
194	 * This adds support for Image Processing Accelerator on GD32F450 and GD32F470 series MCUs
195	 *
196	 * NOTE: IPA on GD32F450 has a bug where the fill operation overwrites data beyond the
197	 * framebuffer. This driver works around it by saving and restoring affected memory, but
198	 * this makes it not thread-safe. GD32F470 is not affected. */
199	#define LV_USE_GPU_GD32_IPA 0
200	
201	/*Use NXP's PXP GPU iMX RTxxx platforms*/
202	#define LV_USE_GPU_NXP_PXP 0
203	#if LV_USE_GPU_NXP_PXP
204	    /*1: Add default bare metal and FreeRTOS interrupt handling routines for PXP (lv_gpu_nxp_pxp_osa.c)
205	    *   and call lv_gpu_nxp_pxp_init() automatically during lv_init(). Note that symbol SDK_OS_FREE_RTOS
206	    *   has to be defined in order to use FreeRTOS OSA, otherwise bare-metal implementation is selected.
207	    *0: lv_gpu_nxp_pxp_init() has to be called manually before lv_init()
208	    */
209	    #define LV_USE_GPU_NXP_PXP_AUTO_INIT 0
210	#endif
211	
212	/*Use NXP's VG-Lite GPU iMX RTxxx platforms*/
213	#define LV_USE_GPU_NXP_VG_LITE 0
214	
215	/*Use SWM341's DMA2D GPU*/
216	#define LV_USE_GPU_SWM341_DMA2D 0
217	#if LV_USE_GPU_SWM341_DMA2D
218	    #define LV_GPU_SWM341_DMA2D_INCLUDE "SWM341.h"
219	#endif
220	
221	/*=======================
222	 * FEATURE CONFIGURATION
223	 *=======================*/
224	
225	/*-------------
226	 * Logging
227	 *-----------*/
228	
229	/*Enable the log module*/
230	#define LV_USE_LOG 0
231	#if LV_USE_LOG
232	
233	    /*How important log should be added:
234	    *LV_LOG_LEVEL_TRACE       A lot of logs to give detailed information
235	    *LV_LOG_LEVEL_INFO        Log important events
236	    *LV_LOG_LEVEL_WARN        Log if something unwanted happened but didn't cause a problem
237	    *LV_LOG_LEVEL_ERROR       Only critical issue, when the system may fail
238	    *LV_LOG_LEVEL_USER        Only logs added by the user
239	    *LV_LOG_LEVEL_NONE        Do not log anything*/
240	    #define LV_LOG_LEVEL LV_LOG_LEVEL_WARN
241	
242	    /*1: Print the log with 'printf';
243	    *0: User need to register a callback with `lv_log_register_print_cb()`*/
244	    #define LV_LOG_PRINTF 0
245	
246	    /*1: Enable print timestamp;
247	     *0: Disable print timestamp*/
248	    #define LV_LOG_USE_TIMESTAMP 1
249	
250	    /*Enable/disable LV_LOG_TRACE in modules that produces a huge number of logs*/
251	    #define LV_LOG_TRACE_MEM        1
252	    #define LV_LOG_TRACE_TIMER      1
253	    #define LV_LOG_TRACE_INDEV      1
254	    #define LV_LOG_TRACE_DISP_REFR  1
255	    #define LV_LOG_TRACE_EVENT      1
256	    #define LV_LOG_TRACE_OBJ_CREATE 1
257	    #define LV_LOG_TRACE_LAYOUT     1
258	    #define LV_LOG_TRACE_ANIM       1
259		#define LV_LOG_TRACE_MSG		1
260	
261	#endif  /*LV_USE_LOG*/
262	
263	/*-------------
264	 * Asserts
265	 *-----------*/
266	
267	/*Enable asserts if an operation is failed or an invalid data is found.
268	 *If LV_USE_LOG is enabled an error message will be printed on failure*/
269	#define LV_USE_ASSERT_NULL          1   /*Check if the parameter is NULL. (Very fast, recommended)*/
270	#define LV_USE_ASSERT_MALLOC        1   /*Checks is the memory is successfully allocated or no. (Very fast, recommended)*/
271	#define LV_USE_ASSERT_STYLE         0   /*Check if the styles are properly initialized. (Very fast, recommended)*/
272	#define LV_USE_ASSERT_MEM_INTEGRITY 0   /*Check the integrity of `lv_mem` after critical operations. (Slow)*/
273	#define LV_USE_ASSERT_OBJ           0   /*Check the object's type and existence (e.g. not deleted). (Slow)*/
274	
275	/*Add a custom handler when assert happens e.g. to restart the MCU*/
276	#define LV_ASSERT_HANDLER_INCLUDE <stdint.h>
277	#define LV_ASSERT_HANDLER while(1);   /*Halt by default*/
278	
279	/*-------------
280	 * Others
281	 *-----------*/
282	
283	/*1: Show CPU usage and FPS count
284	 * Requires `LV_USE_SYSMON = 1`*/
285	#define LV_USE_PERF_MONITOR 0
286	#if LV_USE_PERF_MONITOR
287	    #define LV_USE_PERF_MONITOR_POS LV_ALIGN_BOTTOM_RIGHT
288	
289	    /*0: Displays performance data on the screen, 1: Prints performance data using log.*/
290	    #define LV_USE_PERF_MONITOR_LOG_MODE 0
291	#endif
292	
293	/*1: Show the used memory and the memory fragmentation
294	 * Requires `LV_USE_BUILTIN_MALLOC = 1`
295	 * Requires `LV_USE_SYSMON = 1`*/
296	#define LV_USE_MEM_MONITOR 0
297	#if LV_USE_MEM_MONITOR
298	    #define LV_USE_MEM_MONITOR_POS LV_ALIGN_BOTTOM_LEFT
299	#endif
300	
301	/*1: Draw random colored rectangles over the redrawn areas*/
302	#define LV_USE_REFR_DEBUG 0
303	
304	/*Maximum buffer size to allocate for rotation.
305	 *Only used if software rotation is enabled in the display driver.*/
306	#define LV_DISP_ROT_MAX_BUF (10*1024)
307	
308	/*Garbage Collector settings
309	 *Used if lvgl is bound to higher level language and the memory is managed by that language*/
310	#define LV_ENABLE_GC 0
311	#if LV_ENABLE_GC != 0
312	    #define LV_GC_INCLUDE "gc.h"                           /*Include Garbage Collector related things*/
313	#endif /*LV_ENABLE_GC*/
314	
315	/*Default image cache size. Image caching keeps some images opened.
316	 *If only the built-in image formats are used there is no real advantage of caching.
317	 *With other image decoders (e.g. PNG or JPG) caching save the continuous open/decode of images.
318	 *However the opened images consume additional RAM.
319	 *0: to disable caching*/
320	#define LV_IMG_CACHE_DEF_SIZE 0
321	
322	
323	/*Number of stops allowed per gradient. Increase this to allow more stops.
324	 *This adds (sizeof(lv_color_t) + 1) bytes per additional stop*/
325	#define LV_GRADIENT_MAX_STOPS 2
326	
327	/* Adjust color mix functions rounding. GPUs might calculate color mix (blending) differently.
328	 * 0: round down, 64: round up from x.75, 128: round up from half, 192: round up from x.25, 254: round up */
329	#define LV_COLOR_MIX_ROUND_OFS 0
330	
331	/*=====================
332	 *  COMPILER SETTINGS
333	 *====================*/
334	
335	/*For big endian systems set to 1*/
336	#define LV_BIG_ENDIAN_SYSTEM 0
337	
338	/*Define a custom attribute to `lv_tick_inc` function*/
339	#define LV_ATTRIBUTE_TICK_INC
340	
341	/*Define a custom attribute to `lv_timer_handler` function*/
342	#define LV_ATTRIBUTE_TIMER_HANDLER
343	
344	/*Define a custom attribute to `lv_disp_flush_ready` function*/
345	#define LV_ATTRIBUTE_FLUSH_READY
346	
347	/*Required alignment size for buffers*/
348	#define LV_ATTRIBUTE_MEM_ALIGN_SIZE 1
349	
350	/*Will be added where memories needs to be aligned (with -Os data might not be aligned to boundary by default).
351	 * E.g. __attribute__((aligned(4)))*/
352	#define LV_ATTRIBUTE_MEM_ALIGN
353	
354	/*Attribute to mark large constant arrays for example font's bitmaps*/
355	#define LV_ATTRIBUTE_LARGE_CONST
356	
357	/*Compiler prefix for a big array declaration in RAM*/
358	#define LV_ATTRIBUTE_LARGE_RAM_ARRAY
359	
360	/*Place performance critical functions into a faster memory (e.g RAM)*/
361	#define LV_ATTRIBUTE_FAST_MEM
362	
363	
364	/*Export integer constant to binding. This macro is used with constants in the form of LV_<CONST> that
365	 *should also appear on LVGL binding API such as Micropython.*/
366	#define LV_EXPORT_CONST_INT(int_value) struct _silence_gcc_warning /*The default value just prevents GCC warning*/
367	
368	/*Extend the default -32k..32k coordinate range to -4M..4M by using int32_t for coordinates instead of int16_t*/
369	#define LV_USE_LARGE_COORD 0

436	 * - LV_TXT_ENC_ASCII

442

445	#define LV_TXT_LINE_BREAK_LONG_LEN 0
446	
447	/*Minimum number of characters in a long word to put on a line before a break.
448	 *Depends on LV_TXT_LINE_BREAK_LONG_LEN.*/
449	#define LV_TXT_LINE_BREAK_LONG_PRE_MIN_LEN 3
450	
451	/*Minimum number of characters in a long word to put on a line after a break.
452	 *Depends on LV_TXT_LINE_BREAK_LONG_LEN.*/
453	#define LV_TXT_LINE_BREAK_LONG_POST_MIN_LEN 3

455	/*The control character to use for signalling text recoloring.*/
456	#define LV_TXT_COLOR_CMD "#"

458	/*Support bidirectional texts. Allows mixing Left-to-Right and Right-to-Left texts.

461	#define LV_USE_BIDI 0
462	#if LV_USE_BIDI
463	    /*Set the default direction. Supported values:
464	    *`LV_BASE_DIR_LTR` Left-to-Right
465	    *`LV_BASE_DIR_RTL` Right-to-Left

467	    #define LV_BIDI_BASE_DIR_DEF LV_BASE_DIR_AUTO
468	#endif

86	void lv_obj_set_parent(struct _lv_obj_t * obj, struct _lv_obj_t * parent);

94	void lv_obj_swap(struct _lv_obj_t * obj1, struct _lv_obj_t * obj2);

90:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN          = (1L << 0),  /**< Make the object hidden. (Like it wasn't there at all)*/
91:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE       = (1L << 1),  /**< Make the object clickable by the input devices*/
92:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICK_FOCUSABLE = (1L << 2),  /**< Add focused state to the object when clicked*/
93:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_CHECKABLE       = (1L << 3),  /**< Toggle checked state when the object is clicked*/
94:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE      = (1L << 4),  /**< Make the object scrollable*/
95:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLL_ELASTIC  = (1L << 5),  /**< Allow scrolling inside but with slower speed*/
96:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLL_MOMENTUM = (1L << 6),  /**< Make the object scroll further when "thrown"*/
97:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLL_ONE      = (1L << 7),  /**< Allow scrolling only one snappable children*/
98:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLL_CHAIN_HOR = (1L << 8), /**< Allow propagating the horizontal scroll to a parent*/
99:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLL_CHAIN_VER = (1L << 9), /**< Allow propagating the vertical scroll to a parent*/
100:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLL_CHAIN     = (LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLL_CHAIN_HOR | LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLL_CHAIN_VER),
101:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLL_ON_FOCUS = (1L << 10),  /**< Automatically scroll object to make it visible when focused*/
102:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLL_WITH_ARROW  = (1L << 11), /**< Allow scrolling the focused object with arrow keys*/
103:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_SNAPPABLE       = (1L << 12), /**< If scroll snap is enabled on the parent it can snap to this object*/
104:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_PRESS_LOCK      = (1L << 13), /**< Keep the object pressed even if the press slid from the object*/
105:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_EVENT_BUBBLE    = (1L << 14), /**< Propagate the events to the parent too*/
106:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_GESTURE_BUBBLE  = (1L << 15), /**< Propagate the gestures to the parent*/
107:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_ADV_HITTEST     = (1L << 16), /**< Allow performing more accurate hit (click) test. E.g. consider rounded corners.*/
108:    LV_OBJ_FLAG_IGNORE_LAYOUT   = (1L << 17), /**< Make the object position-able by the layouts*/

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-06-28 05:40:18Z)**_

The LVGL version is actually **not** 8.2.0 - looking at the APIs I found (like `lv_disp_load_scr`, the obj tree structure, etc.), this appears to be a development branch between 8.2 and 9.0. The lv_conf.h comment says "v9.0.0-dev" but the `lvgl.h` declares `LVGL_VERSION_MAJOR 8` and `LVGL_VERSION_MINOR 2`. The API signatures match LVGL 8.x conventions (separate style gen functions with selectors, `lv_obj_create(parent)`, etc.). I'll design for the actual API present in the codebase.

Now I have enough information to create a comprehensive implementation plan.

---

## Breakout Game Implementation Plan for LVGL on SAME51

### 1. File Structure and Module Responsibilities

```
src/
  app.c              (MODIFIED) - Remove demo call, init splash screen instead
  app.h              (UNCHANGED)
  game/
    game_common.h    - Shared types, constants, screen dimensions
    splash_screen.c  - Splash screen UI creation and event handling
    splash_screen.h  - Public interface: splash_screen_create(), splash_screen_destroy()
    breakout.c       - Game logic, timer callback, collision, rendering
    breakout.h       - Public interface: breakout_create(), breakout_destroy()
```

**Module responsibilities:**

- **game_common.h**: Central definitions -- display dimensions (480x320), game area bounds, shared enums for game states, forward declarations.
- **splash_screen.c/.h**: Creates an LVGL screen with a title label ("BREAKOUT") and a "Tap to Start" label. Registers a click event on the screen that transitions to the game.
- **breakout.c/.h**: Contains all game logic -- creates game objects on a new screen, runs the game loop timer, handles collisions, manages score/lives, transitions to game-over state and back to splash.
- **app.c**: Modified to `#include "game/splash_screen.h"` and replace the demo widget call with `splash_screen_create()`.

### 2. Data Structures for Game State

```c
/* game_common.h */
#define SCREEN_WIDTH    480
#define SCREEN_HEIGHT   320

#define BRICK_COLS      10
#define BRICK_ROWS      5
#define BRICK_COUNT     (BRICK_COLS * BRICK_ROWS)  /* 50 bricks */

#define PADDLE_WIDTH    80
#define PADDLE_HEIGHT   12
#define PADDLE_Y        (SCREEN_HEIGHT - 30)

#define BALL_SIZE       10

#define GAME_TIMER_MS   20   /* 50 Hz physics, runs within 33ms LVGL refresh */

#define MAX_LIVES       3

typedef enum {
    GAME_STATE_IDLE,       /* Ball stuck to paddle before launch */
    GAME_STATE_PLAYING,    /* Ball in motion */
    GAME_STATE_GAME_OVER,  /* All lives lost */
    GAME_STATE_WIN         /* All bricks destroyed */
} game_state_t;
```

```c
/* breakout.c - internal state structure */
typedef struct {
    /* LVGL objects (pre-allocated, never destroyed during gameplay) */
    lv_obj_t *screen;
    lv_obj_t *paddle;
    lv_obj_t *ball;
    lv_obj_t *bricks[BRICK_COUNT];
    lv_obj_t *score_label;
    lv_obj_t *lives_label;
    lv_obj_t *message_label;   /* "GAME OVER" / "YOU WIN" / "Tap to launch" */
    
    /* Game timer */
    lv_timer_t *game_timer;
    
    /* Physics state (fixed-point: multiply by 16 for sub-pixel precision) */
    int16_t ball_x;           /* Ball center X (actual pixels) */
    int16_t ball_y;           /* Ball center Y (actual pixels) */
    int16_t ball_vx;          /* Ball velocity X (pixels per tick, scaled x16) */
    int16_t ball_vy;          /* Ball velocity Y (pixels per tick, scaled x16) */
    int16_t ball_x_frac;      /* Fractional X accumulator */
    int16_t ball_y_frac;      /* Fractional Y accumulator */
    
    /* Paddle state */
    int16_t paddle_x;         /* Paddle left edge X */
    
    /* Game state */
    game_state_t state;
    uint8_t lives;
    uint16_t score;
    uint8_t bricks_remaining;
    bool brick_alive[BRICK_COUNT];  /* Track which bricks are visible */
} breakout_state_t;

static breakout_state_t g_game;  /* Single static instance - no malloc */
```

**Why fixed-point velocity:** The ball needs smooth diagonal motion at sub-pixel granularity. Using `int16_t` with a 4-bit fractional part (x16 scale) gives adequate precision without requiring floating point (the Cortex-M4F has FPU, but integer is simpler and deterministic for game physics).

### 3. Game Loop Logic (Timer Callback Flow)

```c
static void game_timer_cb(lv_timer_t *timer) {
    breakout_state_t *g = &g_game;
    
    if (g->state != GAME_STATE_PLAYING) return;
    
    /* 1. Read touch input and update paddle position */
    update_paddle(g);
    
    /* 2. Move ball using fixed-point accumulation */
    g->ball_x_frac += g->ball_vx;
    g->ball_y_frac += g->ball_vy;
    int16_t dx = g->ball_x_frac / 16;
    int16_t dy = g->ball_y_frac / 16;
    g->ball_x_frac -= dx * 16;
    g->ball_y_frac -= dy * 16;
    g->ball_x += dx;
    g->ball_y += dy;
    
    /* 3. Collision detection (order matters) */
    check_wall_collision(g);
    check_paddle_collision(g);
    check_brick_collision(g);
    check_ball_lost(g);
    
    /* 4. Update LVGL object positions */
    lv_obj_set_pos(g->ball, g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE/2, g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE/2);
    lv_obj_set_pos(g->paddle, g->paddle_x, PADDLE_Y);
    
    /* 5. Check win condition */
    if (g->bricks_remaining == 0) {
        g->state = GAME_STATE_WIN;
        show_message(g, "YOU WIN!");
    }
}
```

**Timer creation** (in `breakout_create()`):
```c
g_game.game_timer = lv_timer_create(game_timer_cb, GAME_TIMER_MS, NULL);
```

### 4. Collision Detection Approach

**Ball vs Walls (left, right, top):**
```c
static void check_wall_collision(breakout_state_t *g) {
    /* Left wall */
    if (g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE/2 <= 0) {
        g->ball_x = BALL_SIZE/2;
        g->ball_vx = -g->ball_vx;
    }
    /* Right wall */
    if (g->ball_x + BALL_SIZE/2 >= SCREEN_WIDTH) {
        g->ball_x = SCREEN_WIDTH - BALL_SIZE/2;
        g->ball_vx = -g->ball_vx;
    }
    /* Top wall */
    if (g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE/2 <= 0) {
        g->ball_y = BALL_SIZE/2;
        g->ball_vy = -g->ball_vy;
    }
}
```

**Ball vs Paddle:**
```c
static void check_paddle_collision(breakout_state_t *g) {
    /* Only check when ball is moving downward */
    if (g->ball_vy <= 0) return;
    
    int16_t ball_bottom = g->ball_y + BALL_SIZE/2;
    int16_t ball_left = g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE/2;
    int16_t ball_right = g->ball_x + BALL_SIZE/2;
    
    /* AABB overlap test */
    if (ball_bottom >= PADDLE_Y &&
        ball_bottom <= PADDLE_Y + PADDLE_HEIGHT &&
        ball_right >= g->paddle_x &&
        ball_left <= g->paddle_x + PADDLE_WIDTH) {
        
        /* Reflect vertically */
        g->ball_vy = -g->ball_vy;
        g->ball_y = PADDLE_Y - BALL_SIZE/2;
        
        /* Adjust horizontal velocity based on hit position for angle control */
        int16_t hit_pos = g->ball_x - (g->paddle_x + PADDLE_WIDTH/2);
        /* Map hit_pos from [-PADDLE_WIDTH/2, +PADDLE_WIDTH/2] to vx adjustment */
        g->ball_vx = (hit_pos * 48) / (PADDLE_WIDTH/2);  /* Max vx = +/-48 (3 px/tick) */
    }
}
```

**Ball vs Bricks:**
```c
static void check_brick_collision(breakout_state_t *g) {
    for (int i = 0; i < BRICK_COUNT; i++) {
        if (!g->brick_alive[i]) continue;
        
        /* Calculate brick position from index */
        int col = i % BRICK_COLS;
        int row = i / BRICK_COLS;
        int16_t bx = BRICK_MARGIN_LEFT + col * (BRICK_WIDTH + BRICK_GAP_H);
        int16_t by = BRICK_MARGIN_TOP + row * (BRICK_HEIGHT + BRICK_GAP_V);
        
        /* AABB test: ball circle approximated as square */
        int16_t ball_left = g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE/2;
        int16_t ball_right = g->ball_x + BALL_SIZE/2;
        int16_t ball_top = g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE/2;
        int16_t ball_bottom = g->ball_y + BALL_SIZE/2;
        
        if (ball_right >= bx && ball_left <= bx + BRICK_WIDTH &&
            ball_bottom >= by && ball_top <= by + BRICK_HEIGHT) {
            
            /* Destroy brick: hide LVGL object (do NOT delete) */
            g->brick_alive[i] = false;
            lv_obj_add_flag(g->bricks[i], LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
            g->bricks_remaining--;
            g->score += 10;
            update_score_display(g);
            
            /* Determine reflection axis based on penetration depth */
            int16_t overlap_left = ball_right - bx;
            int16_t overlap_right = (bx + BRICK_WIDTH) - ball_left;
            int16_t overlap_top = ball_bottom - by;
            int16_t overlap_bottom = (by + BRICK_HEIGHT) - ball_top;
            int16_t min_overlap_x = (overlap_left < overlap_right) ? overlap_left : overlap_right;
            int16_t min_overlap_y = (overlap_top < overlap_bottom) ? overlap_top : overlap_bottom;
            
            if (min_overlap_x < min_overlap_y) {
                g->ball_vx = -g->ball_vx;
            } else {
                g->ball_vy = -g->ball_vy;
            }
            
            break;  /* Only destroy one brick per tick to avoid tunneling artifacts */
        }
    }
}
```

**Ball lost (falls below paddle):**
```c
static void check_ball_lost(breakout_state_t *g) {
    if (g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE/2 > SCREEN_HEIGHT) {
        g->lives--;
        update_lives_display(g);
        if (g->lives == 0) {
            g->state = GAME_STATE_GAME_OVER;
            show_message(g, "GAME OVER");
        } else {
            /* Reset ball to paddle, wait for tap */
            g->state = GAME_STATE_IDLE;
            reset_ball_to_paddle(g);
            show_message(g, "Tap to launch");
        }
    }
}
```

### 5. Screen Management (Splash -> Game -> Game Over -> Splash)

**Screen transition architecture:**

Each module owns its own `lv_obj_t *screen` created with `lv_obj_create(NULL)` (parentless = screen object). Transitions use `lv_scr_load()` for immediate switch (no animation to save memory).

```
[Splash Screen] --tap--> [Breakout Screen (IDLE state)]
                              |
                              v
                         [PLAYING state]
                              |
                              v (lives=0)
                         [GAME_OVER overlay on same screen]
                              |
                              v (tap)
                         [Splash Screen]  (or restart)
```

**Implementation flow:**

1. `splash_screen_create()`: Creates screen, adds title + instruction labels, registers `LV_EVENT_CLICKED` on the screen object.
2. On click: calls `breakout_create()` which creates game screen and loads it via `lv_scr_load()`. The splash screen is deleted (`lv_obj_del(splash_scr)`) after load.
3. Game over/win: Displays message label overlay on the game screen. Registers click event to either restart (recreate bricks, reset state) or go to splash.
4. Going back to splash: `breakout_destroy()` deletes game timer and game screen, then `splash_screen_create()` creates fresh splash.

**Key API calls:**
```c
/* Create a new screen (not child of anything) */
lv_obj_t *scr = lv_obj_create(NULL);

/* Switch to it */
lv_scr_load(scr);

/* Delete old screen after switching */
lv_obj_del(old_scr);
```

### 6. Touch Input Mapping for Paddle Control

The touch input is already available via the LVGL input device system (touchX, touchY globals are fed to LVGL's indev). However, for the game loop we need direct access without going through LVGL events. The approach:

**Option A (preferred): Read touch globals directly.**

Since `touchX`, `touchY`, `touchDown` are file-scope statics in `app.c`, we need to expose them. Add to `app.h`:
```c
extern uint32_t g_touchX;
extern uint32_t g_touchY;  
extern bool g_touchDown;
```

Then in `app.c` rename the statics to these globals (or add accessor functions).

**Paddle update logic:**
```c
static void update_paddle(breakout_state_t *g) {
    extern uint32_t g_touchX;
    extern bool g_touchDown;
    
    if (g_touchDown) {
        /* Direct mapping: paddle center follows touch X */
        int16_t target_x = (int16_t)g_touchX - PADDLE_WIDTH / 2;
        
        /* Clamp to screen bounds */
        if (target_x < 0) target_x = 0;
        if (target_x > SCREEN_WIDTH - PADDLE_WIDTH) target_x = SCREEN_WIDTH - PADDLE_WIDTH;
        
        g->paddle_x = target_x;
    }
}
```

**Alternative (Option B): Use `lv_indev_get_point()`** to read the current touch position through LVGL's indev API. This is cleaner but adds a function call:
```c
lv_indev_t *indev = lv_indev_get_act();  /* or store reference */
lv_point_t p;
lv_indev_get_point(indev, &p);
```

I recommend Option A for lowest latency and simplest code, since the globals already exist.

### 7. Performance Considerations for the MCU

**Critical performance rules:**

1. **Pre-allocate all LVGL objects at game start.** Never create or delete objects during gameplay. For bricks, create all 50 at init and hide/show them with `LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN`.

2. **Minimize style operations.** Set all styles once during object creation. During gameplay, only call `lv_obj_set_pos()` for the ball and paddle (2 objects per frame). Hidden bricks are skipped by LVGL's renderer.

3. **Disable scrolling on game screen:**
   ```c
   lv_obj_clear_flag(screen, LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE);
   ```

4. **Use `lv_obj_set_pos()` not `lv_obj_set_x()` + `lv_obj_set_y()` separately.** One call triggers one layout update instead of two.

5. **Game timer at 20ms** (50 Hz physics) runs inside the 33ms LVGL refresh period. LVGL's dirty-area tracking means only the ball's old and new positions plus the paddle get redrawn. Bricks are static once rendered.

6. **Ball size = 10x10 pixels.** The dirty area per frame is approximately (10+max_speed*2) x (10+max_speed*2) pixels for the ball, plus paddle width when it moves. Well within the 480x200 display buffer capacity.

7. **No floating point in game loop.** All physics use integer arithmetic with fixed-point scaling.

8. **Score label: use `lv_label_set_text_fmt()`** instead of `sprintf` + `lv_label_set_text()` to avoid an extra buffer.

9. **Memory budget estimate:**
   - 50 brick objects: ~50 * 200 bytes = ~10 KB (lv_obj_t size in LVGL 8.x is ~150-200 bytes)
   - Ball, paddle, labels, screens: ~5 objects * 200 = ~1 KB
   - Game state struct: ~120 bytes
   - Total LVGL heap usage for game: ~11-12 KB (fits well within 48 KB budget)

10. **Disable `LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE` on ball, paddle, and bricks** to prevent LVGL from doing hit-testing on these objects:
    ```c
    lv_obj_clear_flag(obj, LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
    ```

### 8. Brick Layout Calculation for 480x320 Display

**Layout parameters:**

```c
#define BRICK_COLS       10
#define BRICK_ROWS       5
#define BRICK_WIDTH      40    /* pixels */
#define BRICK_HEIGHT     14    /* pixels */
#define BRICK_GAP_H      4    /* horizontal gap between bricks */
#define BRICK_GAP_V      4    /* vertical gap between bricks */
#define BRICK_MARGIN_TOP  30  /* top margin (space for score/lives labels) */
#define BRICK_MARGIN_LEFT 12  /* left margin for centering */
```

**Verification:**
- Total brick+gap width: 10 bricks * 40px + 9 gaps * 4px = 400 + 36 = 436 px
- Left margin: (480 - 436) / 2 = 22 px (or use 12px if you want slightly left-aligned with 32px right margin -- either works)
- Actually with symmetric centering: `BRICK_MARGIN_LEFT = (480 - (BRICK_COLS * BRICK_WIDTH + (BRICK_COLS-1) * BRICK_GAP_H)) / 2 = (480 - 436) / 2 = 22`
- Total brick+gap height: 5 rows * 14px + 4 gaps * 4px = 70 + 16 = 86 px
- Brick area: from y=30 to y=116
- Play area: y=116 to y=320 (204 px for ball to travel)
- Paddle at y=290 (PADDLE_Y = 290)

**Brick colors by row** (for visual appeal, stored as constant array):
```c
static const lv_color_t brick_colors[BRICK_ROWS] = {
    LV_COLOR_MAKE(0xFF, 0x00, 0x00),  /* Row 0: Red */
    LV_COLOR_MAKE(0xFF, 0xA5, 0x00),  /* Row 1: Orange */
    LV_COLOR_MAKE(0xFF, 0xFF, 0x00),  /* Row 2: Yellow */
    LV_COLOR_MAKE(0x00, 0xFF, 0x00),  /* Row 3: Green */
    LV_COLOR_MAKE(0x00, 0xBF, 0xFF),  /* Row 4: Cyan */
};
```

**Brick creation loop:**
```c
for (int row = 0; row < BRICK_ROWS; row++) {
    for (int col = 0; col < BRICK_COLS; col++) {
        int idx = row * BRICK_COLS + col;
        int16_t x = BRICK_MARGIN_LEFT + col * (BRICK_WIDTH + BRICK_GAP_H);
        int16_t y = BRICK_MARGIN_TOP + row * (BRICK_HEIGHT + BRICK_GAP_V);
        
        g->bricks[idx] = lv_obj_create(g->screen);
        lv_obj_set_size(g->bricks[idx], BRICK_WIDTH, BRICK_HEIGHT);
        lv_obj_set_pos(g->bricks[idx], x, y);
        lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(g->bricks[idx], brick_colors[row], LV_PART_MAIN);
        lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(g->bricks[idx], LV_OPA_COVER, LV_PART_MAIN);
        lv_obj_set_style_radius(g->bricks[idx], 2, LV_PART_MAIN);
        lv_obj_set_style_border_width(g->bricks[idx], 0, LV_PART_MAIN);
        lv_obj_clear_flag(g->bricks[idx], LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE | LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
        
        g->brick_alive[idx] = true;
    }
}
```

### 9. Complete Implementation Sequence

**Step 1: Create `src/game/game_common.h`**
- All #defines for dimensions, speeds, layout
- Include guards, stdint/stdbool includes

**Step 2: Create `src/game/splash_screen.h` and `src/game/splash_screen.c`**
- `void splash_screen_create(void)` -- creates and loads splash screen
- Internal: screen click handler calls `breakout_create()`
- Uses Montserrat 16 for title, Montserrat 14 for instruction

**Step 3: Create `src/game/breakout.h` and `src/game/breakout.c`**
- `void breakout_create(void)` -- creates game screen, all objects, timer; loads screen
- `void breakout_destroy(void)` -- deletes timer, deletes screen
- Internal: `game_timer_cb()`, collision functions, state management
- On game-over tap: call `breakout_destroy()` then `splash_screen_create()`

**Step 4: Modify `src/app.c`**
- Add `#include "game/splash_screen.h"`
- Change touch variables from `static` to non-static (add extern declarations to a header or game_common.h)
- Replace the `#if LV_USE_DEMO_WIDGETS ... #endif` block (lines 211-219) with `splash_screen_create();`

**Step 5: Update build system**
- Add `src/game/splash_screen.c` and `src/game/breakout.c` to the MPLAB X project's `configurations.xml` (source files section)
- Add `src/game/` to the include path in the project settings

**Step 6: Optionally disable demo widgets**
- In `lv_conf.h`, set `#define LV_USE_DEMO_WIDGETS 0` to save flash/RAM (the demo widget code won't be linked)

### 10. Ball Launch and Reset Mechanics

```c
static void reset_ball_to_paddle(breakout_state_t *g) {
    g->ball_x = g->paddle_x + PADDLE_WIDTH / 2;
    g->ball_y = PADDLE_Y - BALL_SIZE / 2 - 1;
    g->ball_vx = 0;
    g->ball_vy = 0;
    g->ball_x_frac = 0;
    g->ball_y_frac = 0;
    lv_obj_set_pos(g->ball, g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE/2, g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE/2);
}

static void launch_ball(breakout_state_t *g) {
    /* Launch upward with slight random angle */
    g->ball_vx = 16;    /* 1 pixel per tick in x16 fixed-point */
    g->ball_vy = -48;   /* 3 pixels per tick upward */
    g->state = GAME_STATE_PLAYING;
    lv_obj_add_flag(g->message_label, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
}
```

The launch is triggered by a tap event (screen click) when `state == GAME_STATE_IDLE`.

### 11. Screen Event Handling

```c
static void game_screen_click_cb(lv_event_t *e) {
    breakout_state_t *g = &g_game;
    
    switch (g->state) {
        case GAME_STATE_IDLE:
            launch_ball(g);
            break;
        case GAME_STATE_GAME_OVER:
        case GAME_STATE_WIN:
            /* Return to splash */
            breakout_destroy();
            splash_screen_create();
            break;
        default:
            break;
    }
}
```

Register on the game screen:
```c
lv_obj_add_event_cb(g->screen, game_screen_click_cb, LV_EVENT_CLICKED, NULL);
lv_obj_add_flag(g->screen, LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
```

### 12. Potential Challenges and Mitigations

| Challenge | Mitigation |
|-----------|-----------|
| Ball tunneling through thin bricks at high speed | Cap ball speed; bricks are 14px tall vs max 3px/tick movement = safe |
| LVGL heap exhaustion with 50+ objects | Budget verified: ~12KB of 48KB. Disable unused widgets in lv_conf.h |
| Touch latency causing jerky paddle | Direct read of `touchX` in timer callback, no smoothing/filtering needed |
| Screen tearing | LVGL's dirty-area approach with 480x200 buffer handles partial updates smoothly |
| `lv_obj_set_pos()` cost | Only 2 calls per frame (ball + paddle). Bricks are static. |
| Collision with multiple bricks simultaneously | Process only first hit per frame to keep physics stable |

```md
# Breakout Game Implementation Plan

## Context

This project is an LVGL demo running on a Microchip SAME51 (Cortex-M4F, 120MHz) with a 480x320 ILI9488 touch display. Currently it shows `lv_demo_widgets()`. We're replacing all existing screens with a splash screen + Breakout game controlled via touchscreen. LVGL version is 8.2.0, RGB565, 48KB LVGL heap, ~30fps refresh.

## File Structure

\```
src/game/
  game_common.h      — Shared constants (display dims, layout, speeds)
  splash_screen.h    — Public: splash_screen_create()
  splash_screen.c    — Splash UI with "BREAKOUT" title + "Tap to Start"
  breakout.h         — Public: breakout_create(), breakout_destroy()
  breakout.c         — Game logic, rendering, collision, game loop timer
\```

## Changes to Existing Files

### src/app.c
- Make `touchX`, `touchY`, `touchDown` non-static (expose for game code)
- Replace the `#if LV_USE_DEMO_WIDGETS ... #endif` block (lines 211-219) with `splash_screen_create()`
- Add `#include "game/splash_screen.h"`
- Remove `#include "../../../third_party/lvgl/demos/lv_demos.h"`

### src/app.h
- Add `extern` declarations for `touchX`, `touchY`, `touchDown`

### igat.X/nbproject/configurations.xml
- Add `../src/game/splash_screen.c`, `../src/game/breakout.c` to source files section
- Add `../src/game/game_common.h`, `../src/game/splash_screen.h`, `../src/game/breakout.h` to header files section

## Game Architecture

### Splash Screen (splash_screen.c)
- Creates a new screen with `lv_obj_create(NULL)`
- Adds centered "BREAKOUT" title (Montserrat 16, bold if available)
- Adds "Tap to Start" instruction label below
- Registers `LV_EVENT_CLICKED` on screen → calls `breakout_create()`
- Dark background (dark blue/black)

### Breakout Game (breakout.c)

**Static state struct** (no malloc):
- `lv_obj_t*` pointers for: screen, paddle, ball, 50 bricks, score/lives/message labels
- Ball position + velocity (integer with x16 fixed-point for sub-pixel)
- Game state enum: IDLE → PLAYING → GAME_OVER / WIN
- Lives (3), score, bricks_remaining count

**Layout (480x320 display):**
- Bricks: 10 cols × 5 rows, each 40×14 px, 4px gaps, centered horizontally, starting at y=30
- Paddle: 80×12 px at y=290, follows touch X directly
- Ball: 10×10 px circle (radius=5)
- Score label: top-left corner
- Lives label: top-right corner

**Game loop** (`lv_timer_create` at 20ms / 50Hz):
1. Update paddle X from `touchX` global
2. Move ball with fixed-point accumulation
3. Check collisions: walls → paddle → bricks
4. Update LVGL object positions (`lv_obj_set_pos` for ball + paddle only)
5. Check win/lose conditions

**Collision:**
- Walls: reflect on boundary contact
- Paddle: reflect Y, adjust ball vx based on hit offset from paddle center (angle control)
- Bricks: AABB test, hide brick with `LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN`, reflect based on penetration axis
- Ball lost below screen: decrement lives, reset ball to paddle (IDLE state)

**Screen flow:**
- Tap in IDLE → launch ball upward-right
- All bricks gone → show "YOU WIN!", tap → back to splash
- Lives = 0 → show "GAME OVER", tap → back to splash

### Performance Strategy
- All 50 brick objects pre-allocated at start, hidden/shown (never created/deleted mid-game)
- Only ball + paddle positions change per frame (2 × `lv_obj_set_pos` calls)
- All objects have `LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE` and `LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE` cleared
- Integer-only physics (no FPU use in game loop)
- Score uses `lv_label_set_text_fmt()` to avoid buffer allocation
- Estimated LVGL heap usage: ~12KB of 48KB available

## Verification

1. Build the project in MPLAB X (XC32 compiler) — should compile without errors
2. Flash to SAME51 IGAT board
3. Verify: splash screen appears on boot with title and tap instruction
4. Tap screen → game starts, paddle follows touch X
5. Ball bounces off walls, paddle, and bricks
6. Bricks disappear on hit, score increments
7. Ball falling below paddle decrements lives
8. Lives=0 shows game over, tap returns to splash
9. All bricks cleared shows win message

```

#include "splash_screen.h"
#include "breakout.h"
#include "game_common.h"
#include "../../../../third_party/lvgl/src/lvgl.h"

static lv_obj_t *splash_scr;

static void splash_click_cb(lv_event_t *e)
{
    (void)e;
    lv_obj_t *old_scr = splash_scr;
    splash_scr = NULL;
    breakout_create();
    lv_obj_del(old_scr);
}

void splash_screen_create(void)
{
    splash_scr = lv_obj_create(NULL);
    lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(splash_scr, lv_color_make(0x00, 0x10, 0x30), LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(splash_scr, LV_OPA_COVER, LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_clear_flag(splash_scr, LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE);

    lv_obj_t *title = lv_label_create(splash_scr);
    lv_label_set_text(title, "BREAKOUT");
    lv_obj_set_style_text_color(title, lv_color_white(), LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_set_style_text_font(title, &lv_font_montserrat_16, LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_align(title, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, -30);

    lv_obj_t *instr = lv_label_create(splash_scr);
    lv_label_set_text(instr, "Tap to Start");
    lv_obj_set_style_text_color(instr, lv_color_make(0x80, 0xC0, 0xFF), LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_set_style_text_font(instr, &lv_font_montserrat_14, LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_align(instr, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 20);

    lv_obj_add_flag(splash_scr, LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
    lv_obj_add_event_cb(splash_scr, splash_click_cb, LV_EVENT_CLICKED, NULL);

    lv_scr_load(splash_scr);
}

```

```c
#include "breakout.h"
#include "splash_screen.h"
#include "game_common.h"
#include "../../src/app.h"
#include "../../../../third_party/lvgl/src/lvgl.h"

static const lv_color_t brick_colors[BRICK_ROWS] = {
    {.full = 0xF800},  /* Red */
    {.full = 0xFD20},  /* Orange */
    {.full = 0xFFE0},  /* Yellow */
    {.full = 0x07E0},  /* Green */
    {.full = 0x05FF},  /* Cyan */
};

typedef struct {
    lv_obj_t *screen;
    lv_obj_t *paddle;
    lv_obj_t *ball;
    lv_obj_t *bricks[BRICK_COUNT];
    lv_obj_t *score_label;
    lv_obj_t *lives_label;
    lv_obj_t *message_label;

    lv_timer_t *game_timer;

    int16_t ball_x;
    int16_t ball_y;
    int16_t ball_vx;
    int16_t ball_vy;
    int16_t ball_x_frac;
    int16_t ball_y_frac;

    int16_t paddle_x;

    game_state_t state;
    uint8_t lives;
    uint16_t score;
    uint8_t bricks_remaining;
    bool brick_alive[BRICK_COUNT];
} breakout_state_t;

static breakout_state_t g_game;

static void reset_ball_to_paddle(breakout_state_t *g)
{
    g->ball_x = g->paddle_x + PADDLE_WIDTH / 2;
    g->ball_y = PADDLE_Y - BALL_SIZE / 2 - 1;
    g->ball_vx = 0;
    g->ball_vy = 0;
    g->ball_x_frac = 0;
    g->ball_y_frac = 0;
    lv_obj_set_pos(g->ball, g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE / 2, g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE / 2);
}

static void update_score_display(breakout_state_t *g)
{
    lv_label_set_text_fmt(g->score_label, "Score: %d", g->score);
}

static void update_lives_display(breakout_state_t *g)
{
    lv_label_set_text_fmt(g->lives_label, "Lives: %d", g->lives);
}

static void show_message(breakout_state_t *g, const char *msg)
{
    lv_label_set_text(g->message_label, msg);
    lv_obj_clear_flag(g->message_label, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
}

static void hide_message(breakout_state_t *g)
{
    lv_obj_add_flag(g->message_label, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
}

static void update_paddle(breakout_state_t *g)
{
    if (touchDown) {
        int16_t target_x = (int16_t)touchX - PADDLE_WIDTH / 2;
        if (target_x < 0) target_x = 0;
        if (target_x > SCREEN_WIDTH - PADDLE_WIDTH) target_x = SCREEN_WIDTH - PADDLE_WIDTH;
        g->paddle_x = target_x;
    }
}

static void check_wall_collision(breakout_state_t *g)
{
    if (g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE / 2 <= 0) {
        g->ball_x = BALL_SIZE / 2;
        g->ball_vx = -g->ball_vx;
    }
    if (g->ball_x + BALL_SIZE / 2 >= SCREEN_WIDTH) {
        g->ball_x = SCREEN_WIDTH - BALL_SIZE / 2;
        g->ball_vx = -g->ball_vx;
    }
    if (g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE / 2 <= 0) {
        g->ball_y = BALL_SIZE / 2;
        g->ball_vy = -g->ball_vy;
    }
}

static void check_paddle_collision(breakout_state_t *g)
{
    if (g->ball_vy <= 0) return;

    int16_t ball_bottom = g->ball_y + BALL_SIZE / 2;
    int16_t ball_left = g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE / 2;
    int16_t ball_right = g->ball_x + BALL_SIZE / 2;

    if (ball_bottom >= PADDLE_Y &&
        ball_bottom <= PADDLE_Y + PADDLE_HEIGHT &&
        ball_right >= g->paddle_x &&
        ball_left <= g->paddle_x + PADDLE_WIDTH) {

        g->ball_vy = -g->ball_vy;
        g->ball_y = PADDLE_Y - BALL_SIZE / 2;

        int16_t hit_pos = g->ball_x - (g->paddle_x + PADDLE_WIDTH / 2);
        g->ball_vx = (hit_pos * 48) / (PADDLE_WIDTH / 2);
    }
}

static void check_brick_collision(breakout_state_t *g)
{
    int16_t ball_left = g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE / 2;
    int16_t ball_right = g->ball_x + BALL_SIZE / 2;
    int16_t ball_top = g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE / 2;
    int16_t ball_bottom = g->ball_y + BALL_SIZE / 2;

    for (int i = 0; i < BRICK_COUNT; i++) {
        if (!g->brick_alive[i]) continue;

        int col = i % BRICK_COLS;
        int row = i / BRICK_COLS;
        int16_t bx = BRICK_MARGIN_LEFT + col * (BRICK_WIDTH + BRICK_GAP_H);
        int16_t by = BRICK_MARGIN_TOP + row * (BRICK_HEIGHT + BRICK_GAP_V);

        if (ball_right >= bx && ball_left <= bx + BRICK_WIDTH &&
            ball_bottom >= by && ball_top <= by + BRICK_HEIGHT) {

            g->brick_alive[i] = false;
            lv_obj_add_flag(g->bricks[i], LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
            g->bricks_remaining--;
            g->score += 10;
            update_score_display(g);

            int16_t overlap_left = ball_right - bx;
            int16_t overlap_right = (bx + BRICK_WIDTH) - ball_left;
            int16_t overlap_top = ball_bottom - by;
            int16_t overlap_bottom = (by + BRICK_HEIGHT) - ball_top;
            int16_t min_overlap_x = (overlap_left < overlap_right) ? overlap_left : overlap_right;
            int16_t min_overlap_y = (overlap_top < overlap_bottom) ? overlap_top : overlap_bottom;

            if (min_overlap_x < min_overlap_y) {
                g->ball_vx = -g->ball_vx;
            } else {
                g->ball_vy = -g->ball_vy;
            }

            break;
        }
    }
}

static void check_ball_lost(breakout_state_t *g)
{
    if (g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE / 2 > SCREEN_HEIGHT) {
        g->lives--;
        update_lives_display(g);
        if (g->lives == 0) {
            g->state = GAME_STATE_GAME_OVER;
            show_message(g, "GAME OVER\nTap to continue");
        } else {
            g->state = GAME_STATE_IDLE;
            reset_ball_to_paddle(g);
            show_message(g, "Tap to launch");
        }
    }
}

static void game_timer_cb(lv_timer_t *timer)
{
    breakout_state_t *g = &g_game;
    (void)timer;

    if (g->state != GAME_STATE_PLAYING) return;

    update_paddle(g);

    g->ball_x_frac += g->ball_vx;
    g->ball_y_frac += g->ball_vy;
    int16_t dx = g->ball_x_frac / 16;
    int16_t dy = g->ball_y_frac / 16;
    g->ball_x_frac -= dx * 16;
    g->ball_y_frac -= dy * 16;
    g->ball_x += dx;
    g->ball_y += dy;

    check_wall_collision(g);
    check_paddle_collision(g);
    check_brick_collision(g);
    check_ball_lost(g);

    lv_obj_set_pos(g->ball, g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE / 2, g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE / 2);
    lv_obj_set_pos(g->paddle, g->paddle_x, PADDLE_Y);

    if (g->bricks_remaining == 0) {
        g->state = GAME_STATE_WIN;
        show_message(g, "YOU WIN!\nTap to continue");
    }
}

static void launch_ball(breakout_state_t *g)
{
    g->ball_vx = BALL_SPEED_X_INIT;
    g->ball_vy = -BALL_SPEED_Y;
    g->state = GAME_STATE_PLAYING;
    hide_message(g);
}

static void game_screen_click_cb(lv_event_t *e)
{
    breakout_state_t *g = &g_game;
    (void)e;

    switch (g->state) {
        case GAME_STATE_IDLE:
            update_paddle(g);
            reset_ball_to_paddle(g);
            launch_ball(g);
            break;
        case GAME_STATE_GAME_OVER:
        case GAME_STATE_WIN:
            breakout_destroy();
            splash_screen_create();
            break;
        default:
            break;
    }
}

static void create_bricks(breakout_state_t *g)
{
    for (int row = 0; row < BRICK_ROWS; row++) {
        for (int col = 0; col < BRICK_COLS; col++) {
            int idx = row * BRICK_COLS + col;
            int16_t x = BRICK_MARGIN_LEFT + col * (BRICK_WIDTH + BRICK_GAP_H);
            int16_t y = BRICK_MARGIN_TOP + row * (BRICK_HEIGHT + BRICK_GAP_V);

            g->bricks[idx] = lv_obj_create(g->screen);
            lv_obj_set_size(g->bricks[idx], BRICK_WIDTH, BRICK_HEIGHT);
            lv_obj_set_pos(g->bricks[idx], x, y);
            lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(g->bricks[idx], brick_colors[row], LV_PART_MAIN);
            lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(g->bricks[idx], LV_OPA_COVER, LV_PART_MAIN);
            lv_obj_set_style_radius(g->bricks[idx], 2, LV_PART_MAIN);
            lv_obj_set_style_border_width(g->bricks[idx], 0, LV_PART_MAIN);
            lv_obj_clear_flag(g->bricks[idx], LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE | LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);

            g->brick_alive[idx] = true;
        }
    }
    g->bricks_remaining = BRICK_COUNT;
}

void breakout_create(void)
{
    breakout_state_t *g = &g_game;

    g->screen = lv_obj_create(NULL);
    lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(g->screen, lv_color_black(), LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(g->screen, LV_OPA_COVER, LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_clear_flag(g->screen, LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE);
    lv_obj_add_flag(g->screen, LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
    lv_obj_add_event_cb(g->screen, game_screen_click_cb, LV_EVENT_CLICKED, NULL);

    create_bricks(g);

    /* Paddle */
    g->paddle_x = (SCREEN_WIDTH - PADDLE_WIDTH) / 2;
    g->paddle = lv_obj_create(g->screen);
    lv_obj_set_size(g->paddle, PADDLE_WIDTH, PADDLE_HEIGHT);
    lv_obj_set_pos(g->paddle, g->paddle_x, PADDLE_Y);
    lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(g->paddle, lv_color_white(), LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(g->paddle, LV_OPA_COVER, LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_set_style_radius(g->paddle, 4, LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_set_style_border_width(g->paddle, 0, LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_clear_flag(g->paddle, LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE | LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);

    /* Ball */
    g->ball = lv_obj_create(g->screen);
    lv_obj_set_size(g->ball, BALL_SIZE, BALL_SIZE);
    lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(g->ball, lv_color_white(), LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(g->ball, LV_OPA_COVER, LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_set_style_radius(g->ball, BALL_SIZE / 2, LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_set_style_border_width(g->ball, 0, LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_clear_flag(g->ball, LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE | LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);

    /* Score label */
    g->score_label = lv_label_create(g->screen);
    lv_obj_set_style_text_color(g->score_label, lv_color_white(), LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_set_style_text_font(g->score_label, &lv_font_montserrat_12, LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_set_pos(g->score_label, 5, 5);
    g->score = 0;
    update_score_display(g);

    /* Lives label */
    g->lives_label = lv_label_create(g->screen);
    lv_obj_set_style_text_color(g->lives_label, lv_color_white(), LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_set_style_text_font(g->lives_label, &lv_font_montserrat_12, LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_align(g->lives_label, LV_ALIGN_TOP_RIGHT, -5, 5);
    g->lives = MAX_LIVES;
    update_lives_display(g);

    /* Message label */
    g->message_label = lv_label_create(g->screen);
    lv_obj_set_style_text_color(g->message_label, lv_color_make(0xFF, 0xFF, 0x00), LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_set_style_text_font(g->message_label, &lv_font_montserrat_16, LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_set_style_text_align(g->message_label, LV_TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER, LV_PART_MAIN);
    lv_obj_align(g->message_label, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 40);

    /* Initial state */
    g->state = GAME_STATE_IDLE;
    reset_ball_to_paddle(g);
    show_message(g, "Tap to launch");

    /* Game loop timer */
    g->game_timer = lv_timer_create(game_timer_cb, GAME_TIMER_MS, NULL);

    lv_scr_load(g->screen);
}

void breakout_destroy(void)
{
    breakout_state_t *g = &g_game;

    if (g->game_timer) {
        lv_timer_del(g->game_timer);
        g->game_timer = NULL;
    }

    if (g->screen) {
        lv_obj_del(g->screen);
        g->screen = NULL;
    }
}

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-06-28 05:49:18Z)**_

However, the compiler might complain about initializing a static const array of union types with designated initializers in C99. Let me use `LV_COLOR_MAKE` which is more portable for LVGL:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-06-28 05:50:09Z)**_

Implementation is complete. Here's what was done:

**New files created:**
- [game_common.h](src/game/game_common.h) — Constants for display, bricks, paddle, ball, and speeds
- [splash_screen.h](src/game/splash_screen.h) / [splash_screen.c](src/game/splash_screen.c) — Dark splash screen with "BREAKOUT" title and "Tap to Start"
- [breakout.h](src/game/breakout.h) / [breakout.c](src/game/breakout.c) — Full Breakout game: 50 pre-allocated bricks (5 rows × 10 cols, color-coded), paddle controlled by touch X, ball with fixed-point physics, AABB collision detection, score/lives tracking

**Modified files:**
- [app.c](src/app.c) — Removed demo includes/calls, added splash screen init, exposed touch globals
- [app.h](src/app.h) — Added `extern` declarations for `touchX`, `touchY`, `touchDown`
- [configurations.xml](igat.X/nbproject/configurations.xml) — Added new source/header files to MPLAB X project

**Game flow:** Boot → splash screen → tap → game starts (ball stuck to paddle) → tap → ball launches → play → game over/win → tap → back to splash.

The game uses integer-only physics at 50Hz (20ms timer), only moves 2 LVGL objects per frame (ball + paddle), and fits well within the 48KB LVGL heap budget (~12KB estimated usage).

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user has a demonstration project for the LVGL graphics library running on a Microchip SAME51 Cortex-M4 MCU (SAM E51 Integrated Graphics and Touch Development Kit). They want to:
   - Remove all current screens (currently shows `lv_demo_widgets()`)
   - Replace with a splash screen that prompts the user to start a game
   - Implement a Breakout game controlled via touchscreen

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Microchip SAME51 Cortex-M4F @ 120MHz, 256KB SRAM
   - LVGL 8.2.0 graphics library (RGB565, 16-bit color)
   - ILI9488 display controller, 480x320 pixels
   - 48KB LVGL heap (LV_MEM_SIZE), 96KB display buffer (480x200)
   - 33ms refresh period (~30fps), game loop at 20ms (50Hz physics)
   - Touch input: single-point capacitive, reports x/y coordinates via globals
   - XC32 compiler, MPLAB X IDE project
   - Fixed-point integer physics (x16 scale) for sub-pixel ball movement
   - LVGL object-based rendering (pre-allocated objects, hidden/shown, never created/deleted mid-game)
   - AABB collision detection for ball vs walls, paddle, and bricks
   - Screen management via `lv_obj_create(NULL)` + `lv_scr_load()` + `lv_obj_del()`

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **src/game/game_common.h** (NEW - shared constants and types)
     ```c
     #ifndef GAME_COMMON_H
     #define GAME_COMMON_H
     #include <stdint.h>
     #include <stdbool.h>
     #define SCREEN_WIDTH    480
     #define SCREEN_HEIGHT   320
     #define BRICK_COLS      10

     #define BRICK_HEIGHT    14

     #define BRICK_GAP_V     4

     #define BALL_SIZE       10

         GAME_STATE_IDLE,
         GAME_STATE_PLAYING,
         GAME_STATE_GAME_OVER,
         GAME_STATE_WIN
     } game_state_t;
     #endif
     ```

   - **src/game/splash_screen.h** (NEW - public interface)

   - **src/game/splash_screen.c** (NEW - splash screen implementation)

     ```c
     #include "splash_screen.h"
     #include "breakout.h"

     #include "../../../../third_party/lvgl/src/lvgl.h"

   - **src/game/breakout.h** (NEW - public interface)
     ```c
     #ifndef BREAKOUT_H

     void breakout_destroy(void);
     #endif
     ```

   - **src/game/breakout.c** (NEW - full game implementation, ~280 lines)
     - Static `breakout_state_t` struct with all game objects, physics state, and game state
     - `init_brick_colors()` - runtime color initialization (red, orange, yellow, green, cyan)
     - `reset_ball_to_paddle()` - positions ball above paddle center
     - `update_paddle()` - reads `touchX` global, clamps paddle to screen bounds
     - `check_wall_collision()` - reflects ball off left/right/top walls
     - `check_paddle_collision()` - reflects ball, adjusts vx based on hit offset for angle control
     - `check_brick_collision()` - AABB test, hides brick, reflects based on min penetration axis
     - `check_ball_lost()` - decrements lives, shows game over or resets to idle
     - `game_timer_cb()` - main game loop (move ball, collisions, update positions, check win)
     - `launch_ball()` - sets initial velocity, transitions to PLAYING state
     - `game_screen_click_cb()` - handles tap in IDLE (launch) and GAME_OVER/WIN (return to splash)
     - `create_bricks()` - creates 50 brick objects in a 10x5 grid
     - `breakout_create()` - creates screen, all objects, timer, loads screen
     - `breakout_destroy()` - deletes timer and screen

   - **src/app.c** (MODIFIED)
