Base directory for this skill: C:\Users\c13999\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\bundled-skills\2.1.195\723188f15371cfc70bce18b5ff6ca61e\verify

**Verification is runtime observation.** You build the app, run it,
drive it to where the changed code executes, and capture what you
see. That capture is your evidence. Nothing else is.

**Don't run tests. Don't typecheck.** Running them here proves you
can run CI — not that the change works. Not as a warm-up,
not "just to be sure," not as a regression sweep after. The time
goes to running the app instead.

**Don't import-and-call.** `import { foo } from './src/...'` then
`console.log(foo(x))` is a unit test you wrote. The function did what
the function does — you knew that from reading it. The app never ran.
Whatever calls `foo` in the real codebase ends at a CLI, a socket, or
a window. Go there.

## Find the change

The scope is what you're verifying — usually a diff, sometimes just
"does X work." In a git repo, establish the full range (a branch may
be many commits, or the change may still be uncommitted):

```bash
git log --oneline @{u}..              # count commits (if upstream set)
git diff @{u}.. --stat                # full range, not HEAD~1
git diff origin/HEAD... --stat        # no upstream: committed vs base
git diff HEAD --stat                  # uncommitted: working tree vs HEAD
gh pr diff                            # if in a PR context
```

State the commit count. Large diff truncating? Redirect to a file
then Read it. Repo but no diff from any of these → say so, stop.
**No repo → the scope is whatever the user named; ask if they
didn't.**

**The diff is ground truth. Any description is a claim about it.**
Read both. If they disagree, that's a finding.

## Surface

The surface is where a user — human or programmatic — meets the
change. That's where you observe.

| Change reaches | Surface | You |
|---|---|---|
| CLI / TUI | terminal | type the command, capture the pane — [example](examples/cli.md) |
| Server / API | socket | send the request, capture the response — [example](examples/server.md) |
| GUI | pixels | drive it under xvfb/Playwright, screenshot |
| Library | package boundary | sample code through the public export — `import pkg`, not `import ./src/...` |
| Prompt / agent config | the agent | run the agent, capture its behavior |
| CI workflow | Actions | dispatch it, read the run |

**Internal function? Not a surface.** Something in the repo calls it
and that caller ends at one of the rows above. Follow it there. A
bash security gate's surface isn't the function's return value — it's
the CLI prompting or auto-allowing when you type the command.

**No runtime surface at all** — docs-only, type declarations with no
emit, build config that produces no behavioral diff — report
**SKIP — no runtime surface: (reason).** Don't run tests to fill
the space.

**Tests in the diff are the author's evidence, not a surface.** CI
runs them. You'd be re-running CI. Tests-only PR → SKIP, one line.
Mixed src+tests → verify the src, ignore the test files. Reading a
test to learn what to check is fine — it's a spec. But then go run
the app. Checking that assertions match source is code review.

## Get a handle

**Check `.claude/skills/` first — even if you already know how to
build and run.** A matching `verifier-*` skill is the repo's
evidence-capture protocol: it wraps the session so a reviewer can
replay what you saw (recording, screenshots). Drive the surface
without it and you get a verdict with no replay.

```bash
ls .claude/skills/
```

- **`verifier-*` matching your surface** (CLI verifier for a CLI
  change, etc.) → invoke it with the Skill tool and follow its
  setup. Mismatched surface → skip that one, try the next. Stale
  verifier (fails on mechanics unrelated to the change) → ask the
  user whether to patch it; don't FAIL the change for verifier rot.
- **`run-*` but no matching verifier** → use its build/launch
  primitives as your handle.
- **Neither** → cold start from README/package.json/Makefile. Timebox
  ~15min. Stuck → BLOCKED with exactly where, plus a filled-in
  `/run-skill-generator` prompt. Got through → note the working
  build/launch recipe so it can become a `verifier-*` skill.

## Drive it

Smallest path that makes the changed code execute:

- Changed a flag? Run with it.
- Changed a handler? Hit that route.
- Changed error handling? Trigger the error.
- Changed an internal function? Find the CLI command / request / render
  that reaches it. Run that.

**Read your plan back before running.** If every step is build /
typecheck / run test file — you've planned a CI rerun, not a
verification. Find a step that reaches the surface or report BLOCKED.

**The verdict is table stakes. Your observations are the signal.**
A PASS with three sharp "hey, I noticed…" lines is worth more than a
bare PASS. You're the only reviewer who actually *ran* the thing —
anything that made you pause, work around, or go "huh" is information
the author doesn't have. Don't filter for "is this a bug." Filter for
"would I mention this if they were sitting next to me."

**End-to-end, through the real interface.** Pieces passing in
isolation doesn't mean the flow works — seams are where bugs hide.
If users click buttons, test by clicking buttons, not by curling the
API underneath.

**Destructive path?** If the change touches code that deletes,
publishes, sends, or writes outside the workspace and there's no
dry-run or safe target, don't drive it live. Verify what you can
around it and say which path you didn't exercise and why.

## Push on it

The claim checked out — that's the first half. Confirming is step
one, not the job. The description is what the author intended;
your value is what they didn't.

You know exactly what changed. Probe *around* it, at the same
surface you just drove:

- **New flag / option** → empty value, passed twice, combined with a
  conflicting flag, typo'd (does the error name it?)
- **New handler / route** → wrong method, malformed body, missing
  required field, oversized payload
- **Changed error path** → the adjacent errors it didn't touch —
  did the refactor catch them too, or only the one in the diff?
- **Interactive / TUI** → Ctrl-C mid-op, resize the pane, paste
  garbage, rapid-fire the key, Esc at the wrong moment
- **State / persistence** → do it twice, do it with stale state
  underneath, do it in two sessions at once
- **Wander** → what's adjacent? What looked off while you were
  confirming? Go back to it.

These aren't a checklist — pick the ones the change points at. Stop
when you've covered the obvious adjacents or hit something worth a
⚠️. A probe that finds nothing is still a step: "🔍 passed `--from ''`
→ clean `error: --from requires a value`, exit 2." That the author
didn't test it is exactly why it's worth knowing it holds.

Still not a test run. You're at the surface, typing what a user
would type wrong.

## Capture

Stdout, response bodies, screenshots, pane dumps. Captured output is
evidence; your memory isn't. Something unexpected? Don't route around
it — capture, note, decide if it's the change or the environment.
Unrelated breakage is a finding, not noise.

Shared process state (tmux, ports, lockfiles) — isolate. `tmux -L
name`, bind `:0`, `mktemp -d`. You share a namespace with your host.

## Report

Inline, final message:

```
## Verification: <one-line what changed>

**Verdict:** PASS | FAIL | BLOCKED | SKIP

**Claim:** <what it's supposed to do — your read of the diff and/or
the stated claim; note any mismatch>

**Method:** <how you got a handle — which verifier/run-skill, or
cold start; what you launched>

### Steps

Each step is one thing you did to the **running app** and what it
showed. Build/install/checkout are setup, not steps. Test runs and
typecheck don't belong here — they're CI's output.

1. ✅/❌/⚠️/🔍 <what you did to the running app> → <what you observed>
   <evidence: the app's own output — pane capture, response body,
   screenshot>

🔍 marks a probe — a step off the claim's happy path, trying to
break it. At least one. A Steps list that's all ✅ and no 🔍 is a
happy-path replay: still PASS, but you stopped at the first half.

**Screenshot / sample:** <the one frame a reviewer looks at to see
the feature — an image for GUI/TUI, code block for library/API;
omit for build/types-only>

### Findings
<Things you noticed. Not just bugs — friction, surprises, anything
a first-time user would trip on. "Took three tries to find the right
flag." "Error message on typo was unhelpful." "Default seems odd for
the common case." "Works, but slower than I expected." Lower the bar:
if it made you pause, it goes here. But the pause has to be yours,
from running the app — not from reading the PR page. A red CI check,
a review comment, someone else's bot: visible to anyone already, and
you relaying it isn't an observation. Claim/diff mismatch, pre-existing
breakage, and env notes also belong.

Each probe gets a line here even when it held — "🔍 empty `--from`
→ clean error" tells the author what *was* covered, which they
can't see from a bare PASS.

Lead with ⚠️ for lines worth interrupting the reviewer for; plain
bullets are context. Empty is fine if nothing stuck out — but nothing
sticking out is itself rare.>
```

**Evidence has to reach the reader.** A file path is only evidence
if the person reading the report can open it. If the `SendUserFile`
tool is in your toolset, you're on a remote surface where they
can't — send the screenshots and recordings with it and let the
report name what you sent. Without it, reference the path and keep
the evidence that matters inline — pane captures and response
bodies travel in the report; a bare path only works when the reader
shares your filesystem.

**Verdicts:**
- **PASS** — you ran the app, the change did what it should at its
  surface. Not: tests pass, builds clean, code looks right.
- **FAIL** — you ran it and it doesn't. Or it breaks something else.
  Or claim and diff disagree materially.
- **BLOCKED** — couldn't reach a state where the change is observable.
  Build broke, env missing a dep, handle wouldn't come up. Not a
  verdict on the change. Say exactly where it stopped +
  `/run-skill-generator` prompt.
- **SKIP** — no runtime surface exists. Docs-only, types-only,
  tests-only. Nothing went wrong; there's just nothing here to run.
  One line why.

No partial pass. "3 of 4 passed" is FAIL until 4 passes or is
explained away.

**When in doubt, FAIL.** False PASS ships broken code; false FAIL
costs one more human look. Ambiguous output is FAIL with the raw
capture attached — don't interpret.


---

The user wants me to add a Space Invaders game to the project, alongside the existing Breakout game. I'll need to:

Let me think about the Space Invaders game design for this platform:
- 480x320 display, 16-bit color, touch input
- LVGL 8.2.0, 48KB heap, 50Hz game loop
- Same constraints as Breakout: pre-allocate objects, integer math, no dynamic allocation

For the game mechanics, I'll use a grid of invaders (around 8 columns by 4 rows to keep memory reasonable), with the player ship at the bottom controlled by touch input. The invaders move horizontally and step down when they hit the screen edges, while the player can shoot bullets upward and the invaders shoot back. I need to be careful about LVGL object count since each object takes up 200-300 bytes in the 48KB heap.

Looking at the object budget, I should have around 45 objects total: 32 invaders, 1 ship, a handful of player and enemy bullets, plus UI labels. That fits comfortably within the constraints. For controls, I'll map touch X position to the ship's horizontal movement and use a tap or cooldown mechanism for firing.

Now I'll set up the implementation structure with header and source files for the game logic.

17	void splash_screen_create(void)
18	{
19	    splash_scr = lv_obj_create(NULL);
20	    lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(splash_scr, lv_color_make(0x00, 0x10, 0x30), LV_PART_MAIN);
21	    lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(splash_scr, LV_OPA_COVER, LV_PART_MAIN);
22	    lv_obj_clear_flag(splash_scr, LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE);
23	
24	    lv_obj_t *title = lv_label_create(splash_scr);
25	    lv_label_set_text(title, "BREAKOUT");
26	    lv_obj_set_style_text_color(title, lv_color_white(), LV_PART_MAIN);
27	    lv_obj_set_style_text_font(title, &lv_font_montserrat_16, LV_PART_MAIN);
28	    lv_obj_align(title, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, -30);
29	
30	    lv_obj_t *instr = lv_label_create(splash_scr);
31	    lv_label_set_text(instr, "Tap to Start");
32	    lv_obj_set_style_text_color(instr, lv_color_make(0x80, 0xC0, 0xFF), LV_PART_MAIN);
33	    lv_obj_set_style_text_font(instr, &lv_font_montserrat_14, LV_PART_MAIN);
34	    lv_obj_align(instr, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 20);
35	
36	    lv_obj_add_flag(splash_scr, LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
37	    lv_obj_add_event_cb(splash_scr, splash_click_cb, LV_EVENT_CLICKED, NULL);
38	
39	    lv_scr_load(splash_scr);
40	}

47	static void reset_ball_to_paddle(breakout_state_t *g)
48	{
49	    g->ball_x = g->paddle_x + PADDLE_WIDTH / 2;
50	    g->ball_y = PADDLE_Y - BALL_SIZE / 2 - 1;
51	    g->ball_vx = 0;
52	    g->ball_vy = 0;
53	    g->ball_x_frac = 0;
54	    g->ball_y_frac = 0;
55	    lv_obj_set_pos(g->ball, g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE / 2, g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE / 2);
56	}
57	
58	static void update_score_display(breakout_state_t *g)
59	{
60	    lv_label_set_text_fmt(g->score_label, "Score: %d", g->score);
61	}
62	
63	static void update_lives_display(breakout_state_t *g)
64	{
65	    lv_label_set_text_fmt(g->lives_label, "Lives: %d", g->lives);
66	}
67	
68	static void show_message(breakout_state_t *g, const char *msg)
69	{
70	    lv_label_set_text(g->message_label, msg);
71	    lv_obj_clear_flag(g->message_label, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
72	}
73	
74	static void hide_message(breakout_state_t *g)
75	{
76	    lv_obj_add_flag(g->message_label, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
77	}
78	
79	static void update_paddle(breakout_state_t *g)
80	{
81	    if (touchDown) {
82	        int16_t target_x = (int16_t)touchX - PADDLE_WIDTH / 2;
83	        if (target_x < 0) target_x = 0;
84	        if (target_x > SCREEN_WIDTH - PADDLE_WIDTH) target_x = SCREEN_WIDTH - PADDLE_WIDTH;
85	        g->paddle_x = target_x;
86	    }
87	}
88	
89	static void check_wall_collision(breakout_state_t *g)
90	{
91	    if (g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE / 2 <= 0) {
92	        g->ball_x = BALL_SIZE / 2;
93	        g->ball_vx = -g->ball_vx;
94	    }
95	    if (g->ball_x + BALL_SIZE / 2 >= SCREEN_WIDTH) {
96	        g->ball_x = SCREEN_WIDTH - BALL_SIZE / 2;
97	        g->ball_vx = -g->ball_vx;
98	    }
99	    if (g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE / 2 <= 0) {
100	        g->ball_y = BALL_SIZE / 2;
101	        g->ball_vy = -g->ball_vy;
102	    }
103	}
104	
105	static void check_paddle_collision(breakout_state_t *g)
106	{
107	    if (g->ball_vy <= 0) return;
108	
109	    int16_t ball_bottom = g->ball_y + BALL_SIZE / 2;
110	    int16_t ball_left = g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE / 2;
111	    int16_t ball_right = g->ball_x + BALL_SIZE / 2;
112	
113	    if (ball_bottom >= PADDLE_Y &&
114	        ball_bottom <= PADDLE_Y + PADDLE_HEIGHT &&
115	        ball_right >= g->paddle_x &&
116	        ball_left <= g->paddle_x + PADDLE_WIDTH) {
117	
118	        g->ball_vy = -g->ball_vy;
119	        g->ball_y = PADDLE_Y - BALL_SIZE / 2;
120	
121	        int16_t hit_pos = g->ball_x - (g->paddle_x + PADDLE_WIDTH / 2);
122	        g->ball_vx = (hit_pos * 48) / (PADDLE_WIDTH / 2);
123	    }
124	}
125	
126	static void check_brick_collision(breakout_state_t *g)
127	{
128	    int16_t ball_left = g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE / 2;
129	    int16_t ball_right = g->ball_x + BALL_SIZE / 2;
130	    int16_t ball_top = g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE / 2;
131	    int16_t ball_bottom = g->ball_y + BALL_SIZE / 2;
132	
133	    for (int i = 0; i < BRICK_COUNT; i++) {
134	        if (!g->brick_alive[i]) continue;
135	
136	        int col = i % BRICK_COLS;
137	        int row = i / BRICK_COLS;
138	        int16_t bx = BRICK_MARGIN_LEFT + col * (BRICK_WIDTH + BRICK_GAP_H);
139	        int16_t by = BRICK_MARGIN_TOP + row * (BRICK_HEIGHT + BRICK_GAP_V);
140	
141	        if (ball_right >= bx && ball_left <= bx + BRICK_WIDTH &&
142	            ball_bottom >= by && ball_top <= by + BRICK_HEIGHT) {
143	
144	            g->brick_alive[i] = false;
145	            lv_obj_add_flag(g->bricks[i], LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
146	            g->bricks_remaining--;
147	            g->score += 10;
148	            update_score_display(g);
149	
150	            int16_t overlap_left = ball_right - bx;
151	            int16_t overlap_right = (bx + BRICK_WIDTH) - ball_left;
152	            int16_t overlap_top = ball_bottom - by;
153	            int16_t overlap_bottom = (by + BRICK_HEIGHT) - ball_top;
154	            int16_t min_overlap_x = (overlap_left < overlap_right) ? overlap_left : overlap_right;
155	            int16_t min_overlap_y = (overlap_top < overlap_bottom) ? overlap_top : overlap_bottom;
156	
157	            if (min_overlap_x < min_overlap_y) {
158	                g->ball_vx = -g->ball_vx;
159	            } else {
160	                g->ball_vy = -g->ball_vy;
161	            }
162	
163	            break;
164	        }
165	    }
166	}
167	
168	static void check_ball_lost(breakout_state_t *g)
169	{
170	    if (g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE / 2 > SCREEN_HEIGHT) {
171	        g->lives--;
172	        update_lives_display(g);
173	        if (g->lives == 0) {
174	            g->state = GAME_STATE_GAME_OVER;
175	            show_message(g, "GAME OVER\nTap to continue");
176	        } else {
177	            g->state = GAME_STATE_IDLE;
178	            reset_ball_to_paddle(g);
179	            show_message(g, "Tap to launch");
180	        }
181	    }
182	}
183	
184	static void game_timer_cb(lv_timer_t *timer)
185	{
186	    breakout_state_t *g = &g_game;
187	    (void)timer;
188	
189	    if (g->state != GAME_STATE_PLAYING) return;
190	
191	    update_paddle(g);
192	
193	    g->ball_x_frac += g->ball_vx;
194	    g->ball_y_frac += g->ball_vy;
195	    int16_t dx = g->ball_x_frac / 16;
196	    int16_t dy = g->ball_y_frac / 16;
197	    g->ball_x_frac -= dx * 16;
198	    g->ball_y_frac -= dy * 16;
199	    g->ball_x += dx;
200	    g->ball_y += dy;
201	
202	    check_wall_collision(g);
203	    check_paddle_collision(g);
204	    check_brick_collision(g);
205	    check_ball_lost(g);
206	
207	    lv_obj_set_pos(g->ball, g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE / 2, g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE / 2);
208	    lv_obj_set_pos(g->paddle, g->paddle_x, PADDLE_Y);
209	
210	    if (g->bricks_remaining == 0) {
211	        g->state = GAME_STATE_WIN;
212	        show_message(g, "YOU WIN!\nTap to continue");
213	    }
214	}
215	
216	static void launch_ball(breakout_state_t *g)
217	{
218	    g->ball_vx = BALL_SPEED_X_INIT;
219	    g->ball_vy = -BALL_SPEED_Y;
220	    g->state = GAME_STATE_PLAYING;
221	    hide_message(g);
222	}
223	
224	static void game_screen_click_cb(lv_event_t *e)
225	{
226	    breakout_state_t *g = &g_game;
227	    (void)e;
228	
229	    switch (g->state) {
230	        case GAME_STATE_IDLE:
231	            update_paddle(g);
232	            reset_ball_to_paddle(g);
233	            launch_ball(g);
234	            break;
235	        case GAME_STATE_GAME_OVER:
236	        case GAME_STATE_WIN:
237	            breakout_destroy();
238	            splash_screen_create();
239	            break;
240	        default:
241	            break;
242	    }
243	}
244	
245	static void create_bricks(breakout_state_t *g)
246	{
247	    for (int row = 0; row < BRICK_ROWS; row++) {
248	        for (int col = 0; col < BRICK_COLS; col++) {
249	            int idx = row * BRICK_COLS + col;
250	            int16_t x = BRICK_MARGIN_LEFT + col * (BRICK_WIDTH + BRICK_GAP_H);
251	            int16_t y = BRICK_MARGIN_TOP + row * (BRICK_HEIGHT + BRICK_GAP_V);
252	
253	            g->bricks[idx] = lv_obj_create(g->screen);
254	            lv_obj_set_size(g->bricks[idx], BRICK_WIDTH, BRICK_HEIGHT);
255	            lv_obj_set_pos(g->bricks[idx], x, y);
256	            lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(g->bricks[idx], brick_colors[row], LV_PART_MAIN);
257	            lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(g->bricks[idx], LV_OPA_COVER, LV_PART_MAIN);
258	            lv_obj_set_style_radius(g->bricks[idx], 2, LV_PART_MAIN);
259	            lv_obj_set_style_border_width(g->bricks[idx], 0, LV_PART_MAIN);
260	            lv_obj_clear_flag(g->bricks[idx], LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE | LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
261	
262	            g->brick_alive[idx] = true;
263	        }
264	    }
265	    g->bricks_remaining = BRICK_COUNT;
266	}
267	
268	void breakout_create(void)
269	{
270	    breakout_state_t *g = &g_game;
271	
272	    init_brick_colors();
273	
274	    g->screen = lv_obj_create(NULL);
275	    lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(g->screen, lv_color_black(), LV_PART_MAIN);
276	    lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(g->screen, LV_OPA_COVER, LV_PART_MAIN);
277	    lv_obj_clear_flag(g->screen, LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE);
278	    lv_obj_add_flag(g->screen, LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
279	    lv_obj_add_event_cb(g->screen, game_screen_click_cb, LV_EVENT_CLICKED, NULL);
280	
281	    create_bricks(g);
282	
283	    /* Paddle */
284	    g->paddle_x = (SCREEN_WIDTH - PADDLE_WIDTH) / 2;
285	    g->paddle = lv_obj_create(g->screen);
286	    lv_obj_set_size(g->paddle, PADDLE_WIDTH, PADDLE_HEIGHT);
287	    lv_obj_set_pos(g->paddle, g->paddle_x, PADDLE_Y);
288	    lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(g->paddle, lv_color_white(), LV_PART_MAIN);
289	    lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(g->paddle, LV_OPA_COVER, LV_PART_MAIN);
290	    lv_obj_set_style_radius(g->paddle, 4, LV_PART_MAIN);
291	    lv_obj_set_style_border_width(g->paddle, 0, LV_PART_MAIN);
292	    lv_obj_clear_flag(g->paddle, LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE | LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
293	
294	    /* Ball */
295	    g->ball = lv_obj_create(g->screen);
296	    lv_obj_set_size(g->ball, BALL_SIZE, BALL_SIZE);
297	    lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(g->ball, lv_color_white(), LV_PART_MAIN);
298	    lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(g->ball, LV_OPA_COVER, LV_PART_MAIN);
299	    lv_obj_set_style_radius(g->ball, BALL_SIZE / 2, LV_PART_MAIN);
300	    lv_obj_set_style_border_width(g->ball, 0, LV_PART_MAIN);
301	    lv_obj_clear_flag(g->ball, LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE | LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
302	
303	    /* Score label */
304	    g->score_label = lv_label_create(g->screen);
305	    lv_obj_set_style_text_color(g->score_label, lv_color_white(), LV_PART_MAIN);
306	    lv_obj_set_style_text_font(g->score_label, &lv_font_montserrat_12, LV_PART_MAIN);
307	    lv_obj_set_pos(g->score_label, 5, 5);
308	    g->score = 0;
309	    update_score_display(g);
310	
311	    /* Lives label */
312	    g->lives_label = lv_label_create(g->screen);
313	    lv_obj_set_style_text_color(g->lives_label, lv_color_white(), LV_PART_MAIN);
314	    lv_obj_set_style_text_font(g->lives_label, &lv_font_montserrat_12, LV_PART_MAIN);
315	    lv_obj_align(g->lives_label, LV_ALIGN_TOP_RIGHT, -5, 5);
316	    g->lives = MAX_LIVES;
317	    update_lives_display(g);
318	
319	    /* Message label */
320	    g->message_label = lv_label_create(g->screen);
321	    lv_obj_set_style_text_color(g->message_label, lv_color_make(0xFF, 0xFF, 0x00), LV_PART_MAIN);
322	    lv_obj_set_style_text_font(g->message_label, &lv_font_montserrat_16, LV_PART_MAIN);
323	    lv_obj_set_style_text_align(g->message_label, LV_TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER, LV_PART_MAIN);
324	    lv_obj_align(g->message_label, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 40);
325	
326	    /* Initial state */
327	    g->state = GAME_STATE_IDLE;
328	    reset_ball_to_paddle(g);
329	    show_message(g, "Tap to launch");
330	
331	    /* Game loop timer */
332	    g->game_timer = lv_timer_create(game_timer_cb, GAME_TIMER_MS, NULL);
333	
334	    lv_scr_load(g->screen);
335	}
336	
337	void breakout_destroy(void)
338	{
339	    breakout_state_t *g = &g_game;
340	
341	    if (g->game_timer) {
342	        lv_timer_del(g->game_timer);
343	        g->game_timer = NULL;
344	    }
345	
346	    if (g->screen) {
347	        lv_obj_del(g->screen);
348	        g->screen = NULL;
349	    }
350	}
351

Perfect! Now I have comprehensive understanding of the architecture. Let me create a detailed summary document:

## Summary: LVGL Game Project Architecture Analysis

Based on thorough examination of the firmware-game project, here's the complete architectural breakdown:

### 1. **Splash Screen Pattern** (`/c/microchip/mchp_fw/mchpgfx/mgsh_lvgl/same5xd5x/firmware-game/src/game/splash_screen.c`)

**Screen Creation:**
- Creates a root LVGL object with dark blue background (`lv_obj_create(NULL)`)
- Disables scrolling on the root screen (`LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE`)
- Applies background color styling: `lv_color_make(0x00, 0x10, 0x30)` with full opacity

**UI Elements:**
- **Title Label**: "BREAKOUT" - centered at y=-30, white color, using Montserrat 16pt font
- **Instruction Label**: "Tap to Start" - light blue color `(0x80, 0xC0, 0xFF)`, Montserrat 14pt, centered at y=20

**Click Handling:**
- Makes the splash screen clickable with `LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE`
- Registers event callback: `lv_obj_add_event_cb(splash_scr, splash_click_cb, LV_EVENT_CLICKED, NULL)`
- Callback (`splash_click_cb`): 
  - Stores old screen reference
  - Sets splash_scr to NULL
  - Calls `breakout_create()` to transition to game screen
  - Deletes the old splash screen object

**Screen Loading:**
- Uses `lv_scr_load(splash_scr)` to make it the active screen

---

### 2. **Shared Constants** (`/c/microchip/mchp_fw/mchpgfx/mgsh_lvgl/same5xd5x/firmware-game/src/game/game_common.h`)

**Display Dimensions:**
```c
#define SCREEN_WIDTH    480
#define SCREEN_HEIGHT   320
```

**Brick Grid Layout:**
```c
#define BRICK_COLS      10              // 10 columns
#define BRICK_ROWS      5               // 5 rows
#define BRICK_COUNT     50              // Total bricks (pre-calculated)
#define BRICK_WIDTH     40
#define BRICK_HEIGHT    14
#define BRICK_GAP_H     4               // Horizontal gap
#define BRICK_GAP_V     4               // Vertical gap
#define BRICK_MARGIN_TOP    30          // Top margin from screen
#define BRICK_MARGIN_LEFT   22          // Left margin from screen
```

**Paddle Dimensions:**
```c
#define PADDLE_WIDTH    80
#define PADDLE_HEIGHT   12
#define PADDLE_Y        290             // Fixed Y position near bottom
```

**Ball:**
```c
#define BALL_SIZE       10              // Square size (renders as circle with radius)
```

**Game Loop Timing:**
```c
#define GAME_TIMER_MS   20              // 20ms timer = ~50 FPS
```

**Game Progression:**
```c
#define MAX_LIVES       3
#define BALL_SPEED_Y    48              // Vertical speed (pixels per 16 fractional units)
#define BALL_SPEED_X_INIT 16            // Initial horizontal speed
```

**Game State Enum:**
```c
typedef enum {
    GAME_STATE_IDLE,                    // Waiting for launch
    GAME_STATE_PLAYING,                 // Ball in motion
    GAME_STATE_GAME_OVER,               // Lost all lives
    GAME_STATE_WIN                      // Cleared all bricks
} game_state_t;
```

---

### 3. **Breakout Game Patterns** (`/c/microchip/mchp_fw/mchpgfx/mgsh_lvgl/same5xd5x/firmware-game/src/game/breakout.c`)

#### **Object Pre-allocation Pattern:**

Static state struct holds all game objects:
```c
typedef struct {
    lv_obj_t *screen;
    lv_obj_t *paddle;
    lv_obj_t *ball;
    lv_obj_t *bricks[BRICK_COUNT];      // Pre-allocated array of 50 brick objects
    lv_obj_t *score_label;
    lv_obj_t *lives_label;
    lv_obj_t *message_label;
    
    lv_timer_t *game_timer;             // Timer handle
    
    int16_t ball_x, ball_y;             // Ball position (pixels)
    int16_t ball_vx, ball_vy;           // Ball velocity (pixels per 16 fractional units)
    int16_t ball_x_frac, ball_y_frac;   // Fractional movement accumulators
    
    int16_t paddle_x;                   // Paddle X position
    
    game_state_t state;
    uint8_t lives;
    uint16_t score;
    uint8_t bricks_remaining;
    bool brick_alive[BRICK_COUNT];      // Tracks brick destruction state
} breakout_state_t;

static breakout_state_t g_game;         // Global instance
```

#### **Game Timer Loop** (`game_timer_cb`):

Runs every GAME_TIMER_MS (20ms):
1. Early exit if state != GAME_STATE_PLAYING
2. **Update paddle**: Reads `touchDown` and `touchX` globals from app.h
3. **Update ball physics**: 
   - Accumulates fractional movement: `ball_x_frac += ball_vx`
   - Converts to pixel movements: `dx = ball_x_frac / 16`
   - Clears accumulated fraction: `ball_x_frac -= dx * 16`
   - Updates position: `ball_x += dx`
4. **Collision detection** (in order):
   - `check_wall_collision()` - bounce off left/right/top edges
   - `check_paddle_collision()` - bounce off paddle with velocity variation
   - `check_brick_collision()` - destroy bricks and bounce
   - `check_ball_lost()` - detect ball below screen
5. **Position rendering**: Updates LVGL object positions

#### **Collision Detection:**

**Wall Collision:**
- Left/right: X boundaries at 0 and SCREEN_WIDTH
- Top: Y boundary at 0
- Bounces are simple velocity inversion: `vx = -vx` or `vy = -vy`

**Paddle Collision:**
- Only checked when `ball_vy > 0` (descending)
- Bounding box: paddle at Y=PADDLE_Y, width=PADDLE_WIDTH, height=PADDLE_HEIGHT
- Ball hits paddle if: 
  - Ball bottom in range [PADDLE_Y, PADDLE_Y + PADDLE_HEIGHT]
  - Ball horizontal center within paddle width
- Hit position calculates horizontal angle:
  - `hit_pos = ball_x - (paddle_x + PADDLE_WIDTH/2)` - center-relative position
  - `ball_vx = (hit_pos * 48) / (PADDLE_WIDTH/2)` - scales hit position to velocity range

**Brick Collision:**
- Loops through all 50 brick slots
- Skips dead bricks: `if (!brick_alive[i]) continue`
- Calculates brick position from grid: 
  ```c
  col = i % BRICK_COLS; row = i / BRICK_COLS;
  bx = BRICK_MARGIN_LEFT + col * (BRICK_WIDTH + BRICK_GAP_H);
  by = BRICK_MARGIN_TOP + row * (BRICK_HEIGHT + BRICK_GAP_V);
  ```
- AABB collision: checks if ball overlaps brick bounds
- On collision:
  - Marks brick dead: `brick_alive[i] = false`
  - Hides object: `lv_obj_add_flag(bricks[i], LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN)`
  - Decrements counter: `bricks_remaining--`
  - Awards points: `score += 10`
  - Determines bounce direction by overlap analysis:
    - Calculates overlaps on all 4 sides
    - Finds minimum horizontal vs vertical overlap
    - If horizontal overlap smaller → bounce X
    - If vertical overlap smaller → bounce Y
  - Breaks after first collision (only one brick per frame)

#### **State Management:**

- **IDLE**: Ball attached to paddle, message "Tap to launch"
  - On click: runs `launch_ball()` → sets velocities, hides message, enters PLAYING
  
- **PLAYING**: Active gameplay
  - Timer loop executes collision checks
  - Paddle follows touch input
  
- **GAME_OVER**: Lost all lives
  - Message: "GAME OVER\nTap to continue"
  - On click: calls `breakout_destroy()` then `splash_screen_create()` (loop to splash)
  
- **WIN**: Cleared all bricks
  - Message: "YOU WIN!\nTap to continue"
  - On click: same as GAME_OVER

- **Ball Lost**: Ball exits bottom of screen
  - Decrements lives
  - If lives == 0 → GAME_OVER
  - Otherwise → IDLE state, resets ball to paddle, shows "Tap to launch"

#### **Screen Creation** (`breakout_create`):

1. Initializes brick colors array (5 colors for 5 rows: red, orange, yellow, green, cyan)
2. Creates root screen object (black background)
3. Pre-creates all 50 brick objects:
   - Calculates position from grid
   - Sets color from `brick_colors[row]`
   - Rounds corners with 2px radius
   - Disables scrolling and clicking
   - Initializes `brick_alive[i] = true`
4. Creates paddle:
   - White, 80x12, centered horizontally
   - 4px rounded corners
5. Creates ball:
   - White, 10x10
   - 5px radius for circular appearance
6. Creates 3 UI labels:
   - Score (top-left): "Score: 0"
   - Lives (top-right): "Lives: 3"
   - Message (center): "Tap to launch" with yellow text
7. Registers screen click callback for state transitions
8. Creates repeating timer: `lv_timer_create(game_timer_cb, GAME_TIMER_MS, NULL)`
9. Loads screen: `lv_scr_load(g_screen)`

#### **Screen Destruction** (`breakout_destroy`):

1. Deletes timer if exists: `lv_timer_del(g_game.game_timer)`
2. Deletes screen if exists: `lv_obj_del(g_game.screen)` (cascades to all children)
3. Nullifies pointers for safety

---

### 4. **Touch Input Integration** (`/c/microchip/mchp_fw/mchpgfx/mgsh_lvgl/same5xd5x/firmware-game/src/app.h`)

**Extern globals (lines 173-175):**
```c
extern uint32_t touchX;      // Touch X coordinate (0-480)
extern uint32_t touchY;      // Touch Y coordinate (0-320)
extern bool touchDown;       // Touch state: true=pressed, false=released
```

**Implementation in app.c (lines 77-78):**
```c
uint32_t touchX = 0, touchY = 0;
bool touchDown = false;
```

**Touch Event Handlers (app.c):**
- `touchDownHandler()`: Updates X/Y, sets touchDown=true
- `touchUpHandler()`: Updates X/Y, sets touchDown=false
- `touchMoveHandler()`: Updates X/Y, keeps touchDown=true

**Usage in Breakout:**
- `update_paddle()` function reads `touchDown` and `touchX` to position paddle:
  ```c
  if (touchDown) {
      int16_t target_x = touchX - PADDLE_WIDTH / 2;
      if (target_x < 0) target_x = 0;
      if (target_x > SCREEN_WIDTH - PADDLE_WIDTH) 
          target_x = SCREEN_WIDTH - PADDLE_WIDTH;
      paddle_x = target_x;
  }
  ```

---

### 5. **MPLAB X Project Configuration** (`/c/microchip/mchp_fw/mchpgfx/mgsh_lvgl/same5xd5x/firmware-game/igat.X/nbproject/configurations.xml`)

**Game Files in Header Section (lines 248-252):**
```xml

  <itemPath>../src/game/game_common.h</itemPath>
  <itemPath>../src/game/splash_screen.h</itemPath>
  <itemPath>../src/game/breakout.h</itemPath>
</logicalFolder>
```

**Game Files in Source Section (lines 755-756):**
```xml
<itemPath>../src/game/splash_screen.c</itemPath>
<itemPath>../src/game/breakout.c</itemPath>
```

**Project Configuration:**
- **Target Device**: ATSAME51J20A (ARM Cortex-M4F)
- **Toolchain**: XC32 (Microchip C Compiler) v4.35
- **Platform**: 3 (ARM)
- **Display**: ILI9488 RGB565 (480x320, 16-bit color)
- **Configuration Name**: ili9488_rgb565_e51_igat

**Include Directories (line 845-846):**
```
../src;../src/config/ili9488_rgb565_e51_igat;../src/packs/ATSAME51J20A_DFP;
../src/packs/CMSIS/;../src/packs/CMSIS/CMSIS/Core/Include;
../src/third_party/rtos/FreeRTOS/Source/include;
../src/third_party/rtos/FreeRTOS/Source/portable/GCC/SAM/ARM_CM4F
```

**Optimization Level**: -O1 (minimal optimization for debugging)

---

### 6. **Application Initialization Flow** (`app.c`)

1. **APP_Initialize()**: Sets state to APP_STATE_INIT
2. **APP_Tasks() - INIT state**:
   - Registers GFX blit callback
   - Initializes LVGL library: `lv_init()`
   - Creates display buffer: 480x200 pixels (scratch buffer)
   - Registers display driver with LVGL
   - Registers touch input listener (3 handlers for down/up/move)
   - Initializes LVGL input device: `lv_indev_drv_register()`
   - Creates 10ms periodic timer for LVGL tick: `SYS_TIME_CallbackRegisterMS()`
   - **Calls `splash_screen_create()`** to show splash screen
   - Transitions to APP_STATE_SERVICE_TASKS

3. **APP_Tasks() - SERVICE_TASKS state**:
   - Polls GFX driver status
   - When ready (status == 0): calls `lv_task_handler()` to process LVGL events and timers

**Global References from app.c:**
- Lines 77-78: Defines touchX, touchY, touchDown globals used by breakout.c
- Line 79: Timer handle for LVGL tick callback
- Line 76: Input listener registration

---

### **Key Architectural Insights**

1. **Stateful Game Object**: Uses a static `breakout_state_t` struct containing all game state, positions, and object references for efficient memory layout

2. **Fractional Physics**: Ball movement uses 16x fractional unit accumulation (`ball_x_frac / 16`) to enable smooth physics on integer display coordinates

3. **Pre-allocated Arrays**: All 50 brick objects created upfront in `breakout_create()`, then shown/hidden rather than created/destroyed during gameplay

4. **Collision Priority**: Wall → Paddle → Brick → Lost detection ensures proper handling order

5. **Simple Overlap Detection**: Brick collision uses minimal-overlap direction determination for realistic bounce angles

6. **Touch Integration**: Simple extern globals allow direct access to touch coordinates without callback overhead during game loop

7. **Timer-Driven Update**: 20ms LVGL timer runs game logic independent of display refresh rate

8. **Clean State Transitions**: Splash → Game → Game Over/Win → Splash using create/destroy pattern

---

**File Paths Summary:**
- Game logic: `/c/microchip/mchp_fw/mchpgfx/mgsh_lvgl/same5xd5x/firmware-game/src/game/`
- App core: `/c/microchip/mchp_fw/mchpgfx/mgsh_lvgl/same5xd5x/firmware-game/src/app.c` & `app.h`
- Project config: `/c/microchip/mchp_fw/mchpgfx/mgsh_lvgl/same5xd5x/firmware-game/igat.X/nbproject/configurations.xml`

## Space Invaders Design Plan for LVGL 8.2.0 on SAME51

### Architecture Answers

**1. LVGL Object Count**

| Category | Count | Notes |
|----------|-------|-------|
| Screen | 1 | Root container |
| Aliens | 32 | 8 cols x 4 rows, hide when destroyed |
| Player ship | 1 | Moved via touch X |
| Player bullet | 1 | One at a time, hidden when inactive |
| Alien bullets | 3 | Max 3 active, hidden when inactive |
| Score label | 1 | Top-left |
| Lives label | 1 | Top-right |
| Level label | 1 | Top-center (optional, could omit) |
| Message label | 1 | Center, for game over/win/start |
| **Total** | **42** | |

**Memory estimate:** Each `lv_obj_t` in LVGL 8.2 is approximately 200-280 bytes (base object with styles). Labels add ~100 bytes more for text buffer overhead.
- 42 objects x ~250 bytes average = ~10,500 bytes
- Style data, timer, misc overhead: ~1,500 bytes
- **Total estimated: ~12KB** (same ballpark as Breakout's 50 bricks+paddle+ball)

This stays well within the 48KB heap (the rest is used by LVGL core, display buffers, and the screen object tree overhead).

**2. Movement: Step-based**

Authentic Space Invaders used discrete steps. This is also CPU-friendlier on Cortex-M4 -- fewer position updates per frame. The aliens advance by a fixed pixel step every N game ticks, where N decreases as aliens are destroyed.

**3. Alien firing: Timer-based random selection**

A frame counter accumulates; when it hits a threshold, one living alien in the bottom-most occupied row of each column is selected randomly to fire. This prevents aliens from firing through each other.

**4. Collision detection: AABB (axis-aligned bounding box)**

Same approach as `breakout.c` -- compare bullet rectangle against each alien rectangle (or player rectangle). Since we only have 1 player bullet checking against 32 aliens and 3 alien bullets checking against 1 player, this is trivial computation.

**5. Speed scaling**

`move_interval = BASE_INTERVAL - (ALIEN_TOTAL - aliens_alive) * SPEED_FACTOR`
Clamped to a minimum interval. As aliens die, the step interval shrinks, making remaining aliens faster.

**6. State struct -- see full definition below**

---

### Constants

```c
/* Grid dimensions */
#define SI_ALIEN_COLS           8
#define SI_ALIEN_ROWS           4
#define SI_ALIEN_COUNT          (SI_ALIEN_COLS * SI_ALIEN_ROWS)  /* 32 */

/* Alien sizes and spacing */
#define SI_ALIEN_WIDTH          28
#define SI_ALIEN_HEIGHT         20
#define SI_ALIEN_GAP_H          6     /* horizontal gap between aliens */
#define SI_ALIEN_GAP_V          6     /* vertical gap between aliens */
#define SI_ALIEN_MARGIN_TOP     35    /* below score bar */
#define SI_ALIEN_MARGIN_LEFT    34    /* centering: (480 - 8*(28+6) + 6) / 2 = 34 */

/* Player ship */
#define SI_SHIP_WIDTH           40
#define SI_SHIP_HEIGHT          20
#define SI_SHIP_Y               285   /* bottom of play area */

/* Bullets */
#define SI_BULLET_WIDTH         4
#define SI_BULLET_HEIGHT        10
#define SI_PLAYER_BULLET_SPEED  6     /* pixels per tick upward */
#define SI_ALIEN_BULLET_SPEED   4     /* pixels per tick downward */
#define SI_MAX_ALIEN_BULLETS    3

/* Movement */
#define SI_ALIEN_STEP_X         4     /* pixels per horizontal step */
#define SI_ALIEN_DROP_Y         12    /* pixels to drop when reversing */
#define SI_BASE_MOVE_TICKS      30    /* ticks between moves at full grid (600ms) */
#define SI_MIN_MOVE_TICKS       4     /* fastest speed (80ms per step) */
#define SI_SPEED_FACTOR         1     /* ticks reduction per alien killed: (30-4)/31 ~= 0.84, use 1 is close enough */

/* Firing */
#define SI_FIRE_INTERVAL_TICKS  40    /* alien fires every ~800ms base */
#define SI_FIRE_INTERVAL_MIN    15    /* fastest fire rate (~300ms) */

/* Scoring */
#define SI_SCORE_ROW0           40    /* top row (hardest) */
#define SI_SCORE_ROW1           30
#define SI_SCORE_ROW2           20
#define SI_SCORE_ROW3           10    /* bottom row (easiest) */

/* Game rules */
#define SI_MAX_LIVES            3
#define SI_INVASION_Y           (SI_SHIP_Y - SI_ALIEN_HEIGHT)  /* if aliens reach here, game over */
```

---

### Complete State Struct

```c
typedef struct {
    /* LVGL objects -- all pre-allocated */
    lv_obj_t *screen;
    lv_obj_t *aliens[SI_ALIEN_COUNT];
    lv_obj_t *ship;
    lv_obj_t *player_bullet;
    lv_obj_t *alien_bullets[SI_MAX_ALIEN_BULLETS];
    lv_obj_t *score_label;
    lv_obj_t *lives_label;
    lv_obj_t *message_label;

    lv_timer_t *game_timer;

    /* Game state */
    game_state_t state;
    uint16_t score;
    uint8_t  lives;
    uint8_t  aliens_alive;

    /* Alien grid state */
    bool     alien_alive[SI_ALIEN_COUNT];
    int16_t  grid_x;        /* X offset of entire grid (top-left of grid bounding box) */
    int16_t  grid_y;        /* Y offset of entire grid */
    int8_t   grid_dir;      /* +1 = moving right, -1 = moving left */
    uint16_t move_tick_counter;  /* counts up; when >= move_interval, step aliens */
    uint16_t move_interval;      /* ticks between alien steps, decreases as aliens die */

    /* Player ship */
    int16_t  ship_x;

    /* Player bullet */
    int16_t  pbullet_x;
    int16_t  pbullet_y;
    bool     pbullet_active;

    /* Alien bullets */
    int16_t  abullet_x[SI_MAX_ALIEN_BULLETS];
    int16_t  abullet_y[SI_MAX_ALIEN_BULLETS];
    bool     abullet_active[SI_MAX_ALIEN_BULLETS];

    /* Alien fire timing */
    uint16_t fire_tick_counter;
    uint16_t fire_interval;

    /* Simple PRNG state (xorshift16 for deterministic random) */
    uint16_t rng_state;

} space_invaders_state_t;
```

**Struct size estimate:** ~200 bytes (pointers: 42x4=168, int16 fields: ~30 bytes, bools: ~40 bytes, padding). Negligible.

---

### Movement Algorithm for Alien Grid

The alien grid is tracked as a single logical block with an (grid_x, grid_y) offset. Individual alien positions are computed as:

```
alien_screen_x = grid_x + col * (SI_ALIEN_WIDTH + SI_ALIEN_GAP_H)
alien_screen_y = grid_y + row * (SI_ALIEN_HEIGHT + SI_ALIEN_GAP_V)
```

**Step logic (called when `move_tick_counter >= move_interval`):**

1. Compute the leftmost and rightmost living alien columns to find effective grid bounds.
2. Trial-move: `new_grid_x = grid_x + grid_dir * SI_ALIEN_STEP_X`
3. Check if the rightmost living alien in the new position exceeds `SCREEN_WIDTH - SI_ALIEN_WIDTH` or leftmost goes below 0.
4. If hitting an edge:
   - Reverse `grid_dir`
   - Drop: `grid_y += SI_ALIEN_DROP_Y`
   - Do NOT step horizontally this tick (authentic behavior)
5. If not hitting edge:
   - `grid_x = new_grid_x`
6. Update all living alien LVGL object positions.
7. Check if any alien row has reached `SI_INVASION_Y` -- if so, game over.

**Speed scaling:**
```c
move_interval = SI_BASE_MOVE_TICKS - (SI_ALIEN_COUNT - aliens_alive) * SI_SPEED_FACTOR;
if (move_interval < SI_MIN_MOVE_TICKS) move_interval = SI_MIN_MOVE_TICKS;
```

---

### Firing Mechanics

**Player firing:**
- On screen tap (LV_EVENT_CLICKED) while `state == GAME_STATE_PLAYING`:
  - If `pbullet_active == false`: spawn bullet at `(ship_x + SI_SHIP_WIDTH/2, SI_SHIP_Y)`
  - Set `pbullet_active = true`, unhide bullet object
- Each tick: if active, `pbullet_y -= SI_PLAYER_BULLET_SPEED`
- If `pbullet_y + SI_BULLET_HEIGHT < 0`: deactivate, hide

**Alien firing:**
- `fire_tick_counter` increments each tick
- When `fire_tick_counter >= fire_interval`:
  - Reset counter
  - Find a random column that has at least one living alien
  - In that column, find the bottom-most living alien (it fires)
  - Find an inactive alien bullet slot; if all 3 active, skip this cycle
  - Spawn bullet at alien's center-bottom position
  - `fire_interval = SI_FIRE_INTERVAL_TICKS - (SI_ALIEN_COUNT - aliens_alive) / 2` (clamped to minimum)

**PRNG (xorshift16 -- no stdlib dependency, deterministic):**
```c
static uint16_t si_rand(space_invaders_state_t *g) {
    g->rng_state ^= g->rng_state << 7;
    g->rng_state ^= g->rng_state >> 9;
    g->rng_state ^= g->rng_state << 8;
    return g->rng_state;
}
```

---

### Collision Detection

**Player bullet vs. aliens (each tick when pbullet_active):**
```c
for each alien i:
    if not alive: continue
    compute alien_x, alien_y from grid offset + row/col
    AABB test: bullet rect vs alien rect
    if hit:
        alien_alive[i] = false
        hide alien object
        deactivate bullet, hide bullet
        aliens_alive--
        score += row_score[row]
        recalculate move_interval
        if aliens_alive == 0: WIN
        break
```

**Alien bullets vs. player (each tick for each active alien bullet):**
```c
for each alien bullet j (0..2):
    if not active: continue
    abullet_y[j] += SI_ALIEN_BULLET_SPEED
    if abullet_y[j] > SCREEN_HEIGHT: deactivate, hide
    AABB test: alien bullet rect vs ship rect
    if hit:
        deactivate bullet, hide
        lives--
        if lives == 0: GAME_OVER
        else: brief invincibility or just continue
```

---

### Full Function List

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `space_invaders_create(void)` | Public. Allocates screen, all objects, initializes state, creates timer, loads screen. |
| `space_invaders_destroy(void)` | Public. Deletes timer, deletes screen (frees all child objects). |
| `static si_init_state(si_state_t *g)` | Resets all game variables to starting values. |
| `static si_create_aliens(si_state_t *g)` | Creates 32 alien LVGL objects with row-based colors. |
| `static si_create_ship(si_state_t *g)` | Creates player ship object. |
| `static si_create_bullets(si_state_t *g)` | Creates 1 player + 3 alien bullet objects (hidden). |
| `static si_create_ui(si_state_t *g)` | Creates score, lives, message labels. |
| `static si_update_ship(si_state_t *g)` | Reads touchX, updates ship_x (clamped to screen). |
| `static si_update_player_bullet(si_state_t *g)` | Moves player bullet upward, deactivates if off-screen. |
| `static si_update_alien_bullets(si_state_t *g)` | Moves alien bullets downward, deactivates if off-screen. |
| `static si_move_aliens(si_state_t *g)` | Step-based grid movement with edge detection and drop. |
| `static si_alien_fire(si_state_t *g)` | Timer-based random alien selection and bullet spawn. |
| `static si_check_player_bullet_hits(si_state_t *g)` | AABB collision: player bullet vs all living aliens. |
| `static si_check_alien_bullet_hits(si_state_t *g)` | AABB collision: alien bullets vs player ship. |
| `static si_check_invasion(si_state_t *g)` | Checks if aliens reached ship Y level (instant game over). |
| `static si_update_positions(si_state_t *g)` | Syncs all LVGL object positions from state variables. |
| `static si_update_score_display(si_state_t *g)` | Updates score label text. |
| `static si_update_lives_display(si_state_t *g)` | Updates lives label text. |
| `static si_show_message(si_state_t *g, const char *msg)` | Shows center message, unhides label. |
| `static si_hide_message(si_state_t *g)` | Hides center message label. |
| `static si_recalc_speed(si_state_t *g)` | Recalculates move_interval and fire_interval after kill. |
| `static si_find_grid_bounds(si_state_t *g, int16_t *left_col, int16_t *right_col)` | Finds leftmost/rightmost living columns for edge detection. |
| `static si_rand(si_state_t *g)` | 16-bit xorshift PRNG. |
| `static si_game_timer_cb(lv_timer_t *timer)` | Main game loop: dispatches updates based on state. |
| `static si_screen_click_cb(lv_event_t *e)` | Click handler: fire bullet or transition states. |

---

### Game Timer Callback Structure

```c
static void si_game_timer_cb(lv_timer_t *timer) {
    space_invaders_state_t *g = &g_si;
    (void)timer;

    if (g->state != GAME_STATE_PLAYING) return;

    si_update_ship(g);
    si_update_player_bullet(g);
    si_update_alien_bullets(g);

    /* Alien movement on interval */
    g->move_tick_counter++;
    if (g->move_tick_counter >= g->move_interval) {
        g->move_tick_counter = 0;
        si_move_aliens(g);
    }

    /* Alien firing on interval */
    g->fire_tick_counter++;
    if (g->fire_tick_counter >= g->fire_interval) {
        g->fire_tick_counter = 0;
        si_alien_fire(g);
    }

    /* Collision detection */
    si_check_player_bullet_hits(g);
    si_check_alien_bullet_hits(g);
    si_check_invasion(g);

    /* Update LVGL object positions */
    si_update_positions(g);
}
```

---

### Click Callback State Machine

```c
static void si_screen_click_cb(lv_event_t *e) {
    space_invaders_state_t *g = &g_si;
    (void)e;

    switch (g->state) {
        case GAME_STATE_IDLE:
            /* Start game */
            g->state = GAME_STATE_PLAYING;
            si_hide_message(g);
            break;
        case GAME_STATE_PLAYING:
            /* Fire bullet */
            if (!g->pbullet_active) {
                g->pbullet_x = g->ship_x + SI_SHIP_WIDTH / 2 - SI_BULLET_WIDTH / 2;
                g->pbullet_y = SI_SHIP_Y - SI_BULLET_HEIGHT;
                g->pbullet_active = true;
                lv_obj_clear_flag(g->player_bullet, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
            }
            break;
        case GAME_STATE_GAME_OVER:
        case GAME_STATE_WIN:
            /* Return to splash */
            space_invaders_destroy();
            splash_screen_create();
            break;
    }
}
```

### Memory Budget Summary

| Item | Bytes |
|------|-------|
| 32 alien `lv_obj_t` (plain obj, minimal style) | ~7,680 (240 each) |
| 1 ship `lv_obj_t` | ~240 |
| 4 bullet `lv_obj_t` | ~960 |
| 3 label `lv_obj_t` (with text buffers) | ~1,050 |
| 1 screen `lv_obj_t` | ~300 |
| Timer allocation | ~64 |
| Static struct (BSS, not heap) | ~210 |
| Misc LVGL internal (child arrays, style cache) | ~1,500 |
| **Total LVGL heap** | **~11,800** |

This is within the same ~12KB budget as Breakout. The static state struct lives in BSS (not LVGL heap), so it costs 0 heap bytes.

Design a Space Invaders game for an embedded LVGL 8.2.0 project on a Microchip SAME51 Cortex-M4F MCU with a 480x320 ILI9488 touch display (RGB565, 16-bit color).

**Constraints:**
- 48KB LVGL heap total, existing Breakout game uses ~12KB when active
- Integer-only physics (no floating point in game loop)
- All LVGL objects must be pre-allocated (hide/show, never create/delete mid-game)
- Game loop via `lv_timer_create()` at 20ms (50Hz)
- Touch input: `touchX` (uint32_t, 0-480), `touchY` (uint32_t, 0-320), `touchDown` (bool) - extern globals
- Player controls ship X position via touch X coordinate (same as paddle in Breakout)

- Must follow same file pattern: `src/game/space_invaders.h` and `src/game/space_invaders.c`
- Public interface: `space_invaders_create()` and `space_invaders_destroy()`
- Uses `game_common.h` for SCREEN_WIDTH (480), SCREEN_HEIGHT (320), game_state_t enum, GAME_TIMER_MS (20)

**Game Design Requirements:**
- Classic Space Invaders: grid of aliens moves side-to-side, drops down when hitting edge
- Player ship at bottom controlled by touch X
- Player fires bullet on screen tap (one bullet at a time)
- Aliens periodically fire bullets downward (random alien shoots)
- Aliens speed up as fewer remain
- Score tracking, lives (3), game over / win conditions
- Transition: game over/win → tap → back to splash screen

**Layout for 480x320:**
- Alien grid: ~8 cols × 4 rows (32 aliens) - keeps object count reasonable for memory
- Alien size: ~28x20 px with gaps
- Player ship: ~40x20 px at Y=285
- Bullets: 4x10 px (1 player bullet + up to 3 alien bullets active)
- Score top-left, Lives top-right, message center

3. How to handle alien firing: which alien fires, timing
4. Collision detection approach for bullets vs aliens and bullets vs player
5. Speed scaling as aliens are destroyed

52	    g->ball_vy = 0;
53	    g->ball_x_frac = 0;
54	    g->ball_y_frac = 0;
55	    lv_obj_set_pos(g->ball, g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE / 2, g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE / 2);
56	}
57	
58	static void update_score_display(breakout_state_t *g)
59	{
60	    lv_label_set_text_fmt(g->score_label, "Score: %d", g->score);
61	}
62	
63	static void update_lives_display(breakout_state_t *g)
64	{
65	    lv_label_set_text_fmt(g->lives_label, "Lives: %d", g->lives);
66	}
67	
68	static void show_message(breakout_state_t *g, const char *msg)
69	{
70	    lv_label_set_text(g->message_label, msg);
71	    lv_obj_clear_flag(g->message_label, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
72	}
73	
74	static void hide_message(breakout_state_t *g)
75	{
76	    lv_obj_add_flag(g->message_label, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
77	}
78	
79	static void update_paddle(breakout_state_t *g)
80	{
81	    if (touchDown) {
82	        int16_t target_x = (int16_t)touchX - PADDLE_WIDTH / 2;
83	        if (target_x < 0) target_x = 0;
84	        if (target_x > SCREEN_WIDTH - PADDLE_WIDTH) target_x = SCREEN_WIDTH - PADDLE_WIDTH;
85	        g->paddle_x = target_x;
86	    }
87	}
88	
89	static void check_wall_collision(breakout_state_t *g)
90	{
91	    if (g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE / 2 <= 0) {
92	        g->ball_x = BALL_SIZE / 2;
93	        g->ball_vx = -g->ball_vx;
94	    }
95	    if (g->ball_x + BALL_SIZE / 2 >= SCREEN_WIDTH) {
96	        g->ball_x = SCREEN_WIDTH - BALL_SIZE / 2;
97	        g->ball_vx = -g->ball_vx;
98	    }
99	    if (g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE / 2 <= 0) {
100	        g->ball_y = BALL_SIZE / 2;
101	        g->ball_vy = -g->ball_vy;
102	    }
103	}
104	
105	static void check_paddle_collision(breakout_state_t *g)
106	{
107	    if (g->ball_vy <= 0) return;
108	
109	    int16_t ball_bottom = g->ball_y + BALL_SIZE / 2;
110	    int16_t ball_left = g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE / 2;
111	    int16_t ball_right = g->ball_x + BALL_SIZE / 2;
112	
113	    if (ball_bottom >= PADDLE_Y &&
114	        ball_bottom <= PADDLE_Y + PADDLE_HEIGHT &&
115	        ball_right >= g->paddle_x &&
116	        ball_left <= g->paddle_x + PADDLE_WIDTH) {
117	
118	        g->ball_vy = -g->ball_vy;
119	        g->ball_y = PADDLE_Y - BALL_SIZE / 2;
120	
121	        int16_t hit_pos = g->ball_x - (g->paddle_x + PADDLE_WIDTH / 2);
122	        g->ball_vx = (hit_pos * 48) / (PADDLE_WIDTH / 2);
123	    }
124	}
125	
126	static void check_brick_collision(breakout_state_t *g)
127	{
128	    int16_t ball_left = g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE / 2;
129	    int16_t ball_right = g->ball_x + BALL_SIZE / 2;
130	    int16_t ball_top = g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE / 2;
131	    int16_t ball_bottom = g->ball_y + BALL_SIZE / 2;
132	
133	    for (int i = 0; i < BRICK_COUNT; i++) {
134	        if (!g->brick_alive[i]) continue;
135	
136	        int col = i % BRICK_COLS;
137	        int row = i / BRICK_COLS;
138	        int16_t bx = BRICK_MARGIN_LEFT + col * (BRICK_WIDTH + BRICK_GAP_H);
139	        int16_t by = BRICK_MARGIN_TOP + row * (BRICK_HEIGHT + BRICK_GAP_V);
140	
141	        if (ball_right >= bx && ball_left <= bx + BRICK_WIDTH &&
142	            ball_bottom >= by && ball_top <= by + BRICK_HEIGHT) {
143	
144	            g->brick_alive[i] = false;
145	            lv_obj_add_flag(g->bricks[i], LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
146	            g->bricks_remaining--;
147	            g->score += 10;
148	            update_score_display(g);
149	
150	            int16_t overlap_left = ball_right - bx;
151	            int16_t overlap_right = (bx + BRICK_WIDTH) - ball_left;
152	            int16_t overlap_top = ball_bottom - by;
153	            int16_t overlap_bottom = (by + BRICK_HEIGHT) - ball_top;
154	            int16_t min_overlap_x = (overlap_left < overlap_right) ? overlap_left : overlap_right;
155	            int16_t min_overlap_y = (overlap_top < overlap_bottom) ? overlap_top : overlap_bottom;
156	
157	            if (min_overlap_x < min_overlap_y) {
158	                g->ball_vx = -g->ball_vx;
159	            } else {
160	                g->ball_vy = -g->ball_vy;
161	            }
162	
163	            break;
164	        }
165	    }
166	}
167	
168	static void check_ball_lost(breakout_state_t *g)
169	{
170	    if (g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE / 2 > SCREEN_HEIGHT) {
171	        g->lives--;
172	        update_lives_display(g);
173	        if (g->lives == 0) {
174	            g->state = GAME_STATE_GAME_OVER;
175	            show_message(g, "GAME OVER\nTap to continue");
176	        } else {
177	            g->state = GAME_STATE_IDLE;
178	            reset_ball_to_paddle(g);
179	            show_message(g, "Tap to launch");
180	        }
181	    }
182	}
183	
184	static void game_timer_cb(lv_timer_t *timer)
185	{
186	    breakout_state_t *g = &g_game;
187	    (void)timer;
188	
189	    if (g->state != GAME_STATE_PLAYING) return;
190	
191	    update_paddle(g);
192	
193	    g->ball_x_frac += g->ball_vx;
194	    g->ball_y_frac += g->ball_vy;
195	    int16_t dx = g->ball_x_frac / 16;
196	    int16_t dy = g->ball_y_frac / 16;
197	    g->ball_x_frac -= dx * 16;
198	    g->ball_y_frac -= dy * 16;
199	    g->ball_x += dx;
200	    g->ball_y += dy;
201	
202	    check_wall_collision(g);
203	    check_paddle_collision(g);
204	    check_brick_collision(g);
205	    check_ball_lost(g);
206	
207	    lv_obj_set_pos(g->ball, g->ball_x - BALL_SIZE / 2, g->ball_y - BALL_SIZE / 2);
208	    lv_obj_set_pos(g->paddle, g->paddle_x, PADDLE_Y);
209	
210	    if (g->bricks_remaining == 0) {
211	        g->state = GAME_STATE_WIN;
212	        show_message(g, "YOU WIN!\nTap to continue");
213	    }
214	}
215	
216	static void launch_ball(breakout_state_t *g)
217	{
218	    g->ball_vx = BALL_SPEED_X_INIT;
219	    g->ball_vy = -BALL_SPEED_Y;
220	    g->state = GAME_STATE_PLAYING;
221	    hide_message(g);
222	}
223	
224	static void game_screen_click_cb(lv_event_t *e)
225	{
226	    breakout_state_t *g = &g_game;
227	    (void)e;
228	
229	    switch (g->state) {
230	        case GAME_STATE_IDLE:
231	            update_paddle(g);
232	            reset_ball_to_paddle(g);
233	            launch_ball(g);
234	            break;
235	        case GAME_STATE_GAME_OVER:
236	        case GAME_STATE_WIN:
237	            breakout_destroy();
238	            splash_screen_create();
239	            break;
240	        default:
241	            break;
242	    }
243	}
244	
245	static void create_bricks(breakout_state_t *g)
246	{
247	    for (int row = 0; row < BRICK_ROWS; row++) {
248	        for (int col = 0; col < BRICK_COLS; col++) {
249	            int idx = row * BRICK_COLS + col;
250	            int16_t x = BRICK_MARGIN_LEFT + col * (BRICK_WIDTH + BRICK_GAP_H);
251	            int16_t y = BRICK_MARGIN_TOP + row * (BRICK_HEIGHT + BRICK_GAP_V);
252	
253	            g->bricks[idx] = lv_obj_create(g->screen);
254	            lv_obj_set_size(g->bricks[idx], BRICK_WIDTH, BRICK_HEIGHT);
255	            lv_obj_set_pos(g->bricks[idx], x, y);
256	            lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(g->bricks[idx], brick_colors[row], LV_PART_MAIN);
257	            lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(g->bricks[idx], LV_OPA_COVER, LV_PART_MAIN);
258	            lv_obj_set_style_radius(g->bricks[idx], 2, LV_PART_MAIN);
259	            lv_obj_set_style_border_width(g->bricks[idx], 0, LV_PART_MAIN);
260	            lv_obj_clear_flag(g->bricks[idx], LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE | LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
261	
262	            g->brick_alive[idx] = true;
263	        }
264	    }
265	    g->bricks_remaining = BRICK_COUNT;
266	}
267	
268	void breakout_create(void)
269	{
270	    breakout_state_t *g = &g_game;
271	
272	    init_brick_colors();
273	
274	    g->screen = lv_obj_create(NULL);
275	    lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(g->screen, lv_color_black(), LV_PART_MAIN);
276	    lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(g->screen, LV_OPA_COVER, LV_PART_MAIN);
277	    lv_obj_clear_flag(g->screen, LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE);
278	    lv_obj_add_flag(g->screen, LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
279	    lv_obj_add_event_cb(g->screen, game_screen_click_cb, LV_EVENT_CLICKED, NULL);
280	
281	    create_bricks(g);
282	
283	    /* Paddle */
284	    g->paddle_x = (SCREEN_WIDTH - PADDLE_WIDTH) / 2;
285	    g->paddle = lv_obj_create(g->screen);
286	    lv_obj_set_size(g->paddle, PADDLE_WIDTH, PADDLE_HEIGHT);
287	    lv_obj_set_pos(g->paddle, g->paddle_x, PADDLE_Y);
288	    lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(g->paddle, lv_color_white(), LV_PART_MAIN);
289	    lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(g->paddle, LV_OPA_COVER, LV_PART_MAIN);
290	    lv_obj_set_style_radius(g->paddle, 4, LV_PART_MAIN);
291	    lv_obj_set_style_border_width(g->paddle, 0, LV_PART_MAIN);
292	    lv_obj_clear_flag(g->paddle, LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE | LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
293	
294	    /* Ball */
295	    g->ball = lv_obj_create(g->screen);
296	    lv_obj_set_size(g->ball, BALL_SIZE, BALL_SIZE);
297	    lv_obj_set_style_bg_color(g->ball, lv_color_white(), LV_PART_MAIN);
298	    lv_obj_set_style_bg_opa(g->ball, LV_OPA_COVER, LV_PART_MAIN);
299	    lv_obj_set_style_radius(g->ball, BALL_SIZE / 2, LV_PART_MAIN);
300	    lv_obj_set_style_border_width(g->ball, 0, LV_PART_MAIN);
301	    lv_obj_clear_flag(g->ball, LV_OBJ_FLAG_SCROLLABLE | LV_OBJ_FLAG_CLICKABLE);
302	
303	    /* Score label */
304	    g->score_label = lv_label_create(g->screen);
305	    lv_obj_set_style_text_color(g->score_label, lv_color_white(), LV_PART_MAIN);
306	    lv_obj_set_style_text_font(g->score_label, &lv_font_montserrat_12, LV_PART_MAIN);
307	    lv_obj_set_pos(g->score_label, 5, 5);
308	    g->score = 0;
309	    update_score_display(g);
310	
311	    /* Lives label */
312	    g->lives_label = lv_label_create(g->screen);
313	    lv_obj_set_style_text_color(g->lives_label, lv_color_white(), LV_PART_MAIN);
314	    lv_obj_set_style_text_font(g->lives_label, &lv_font_montserrat_12, LV_PART_MAIN);
315	    lv_obj_align(g->lives_label, LV_ALIGN_TOP_RIGHT, -5, 5);
316	    g->lives = MAX_LIVES;
317	    update_lives_display(g);
318	
319	    /* Message label */
320	    g->message_label = lv_label_create(g->screen);
321	    lv_obj_set_style_text_color(g->message_label, lv_color_make(0xFF, 0xFF, 0x00), LV_PART_MAIN);
322	    lv_obj_set_style_text_font(g->message_label, &lv_font_montserrat_16, LV_PART_MAIN);
323	    lv_obj_set_style_text_align(g->message_label, LV_TEXT_ALIGN_CENTER, LV_PART_MAIN);
324	    lv_obj_align(g->message_label, LV_ALIGN_CENTER, 0, 40);
325	
326	    /* Initial state */
327	    g->state = GAME_STATE_IDLE;
328	    reset_ball_to_paddle(g);
329	    show_message(g, "Tap to launch");
330	
331	    /* Game loop timer */
332	    g->game_timer = lv_timer_create(game_timer_cb, GAME_TIMER_MS, NULL);
333	
334	    lv_scr_load(g->screen);
335	}
336	
337	void breakout_destroy(void)
338	{
339	    breakout_state_t *g = &g_game;
340	
341	    if (g->game_timer) {
342	        lv_timer_del(g->game_timer);
343	        g->game_timer = NULL;
344	    }
345	
346	    if (g->screen) {
347	        lv_obj_del(g->screen);
348	        g->screen = NULL;
349	    }
350	}
351

Good - confirmed 48KB LVGL heap. Now I have all the information needed to design the complete Space Invaders implementation. Let me summarize my findings and deliver the design.

---

## Space Invaders Design Plan for LVGL 8.2.0 on SAME51

### Architecture Answers

**1. LVGL Object Count**

| Category | Count | Notes |
|----------|-------|-------|
| Screen | 1 | Root container |
| Aliens | 32 | 8 cols x 4 rows, hide when destroyed |
| Player ship | 1 | Moved via touch X |
| Player bullet | 1 | One at a time, hidden when inactive |
| Alien bullets | 3 | Max 3 active, hidden when inactive |
| Score label | 1 | Top-left |
| Lives label | 1 | Top-right |
| Level label | 1 | Top-center (optional, could omit) |
| Message label | 1 | Center, for game over/win/start |
| **Total** | **42** | |

**Memory estimate:** Each `lv_obj_t` in LVGL 8.2 is approximately 200-280 bytes (base object with styles). Labels add ~100 bytes more for text buffer overhead.
- 42 objects x ~250 bytes average = ~10,500 bytes
- Style data, timer, misc overhead: ~1,500 bytes
- **Total estimated: ~12KB** (same ballpark as Breakout's 50 bricks+paddle+ball)

This stays well within the 48KB heap (the rest is used by LVGL core, display buffers, and the screen object tree overhead).

**2. Movement: Step-based**

Authentic Space Invaders used discrete steps. This is also CPU-friendlier on Cortex-M4 -- fewer position updates per frame. The aliens advance by a fixed pixel step every N game ticks, where N decreases as aliens are destroyed.

**3. Alien firing: Timer-based random selection**

A frame counter accumulates; when it hits a threshold, one living alien in the bottom-most occupied row of each column is selected randomly to fire. This prevents aliens from firing through each other.

**4. Collision detection: AABB (axis-aligned bounding box)**

Same approach as `breakout.c` -- compare bullet rectangle against each alien rectangle (or player rectangle). Since we only have 1 player bullet checking against 32 aliens and 3 alien bullets checking against 1 player, this is trivial computation.

**5. Speed scaling**

`move_interval = BASE_INTERVAL - (ALIEN_TOTAL - aliens_alive) * SPEED_FACTOR`
Clamped to a minimum interval. As aliens die, the step interval shrinks, making remaining aliens faster.

**6. State struct -- see full definition below**

---

### Constants

```c
/* Grid dimensions */
#define SI_ALIEN_COLS           8
#define SI_ALIEN_ROWS           4
#define SI_ALIEN_COUNT          (SI_ALIEN_COLS * SI_ALIEN_ROWS)  /* 32 */

/* Alien sizes and spacing */
#define SI_ALIEN_WIDTH          28
#define SI_ALIEN_HEIGHT         20
#define SI_ALIEN_GAP_H          6     /* horizontal gap between aliens */
#define SI_ALIEN_GAP_V          6     /* vertical gap between aliens */
#define SI_ALIEN_MARGIN_TOP     35    /* below score bar */
#define SI_ALIEN_MARGIN_LEFT    34    /* centering: (480 - 8*(28+6) + 6) / 2 = 34 */

/* Player ship */
#define SI_SHIP_WIDTH           40
#define SI_SHIP_HEIGHT          20
#define SI_SHIP_Y               285   /* bottom of play area */

/* Bullets */
#define SI_BULLET_WIDTH         4
#define SI_BULLET_HEIGHT        10
#define SI_PLAYER_BULLET_SPEED  6     /* pixels per tick upward */
#define SI_ALIEN_BULLET_SPEED   4     /* pixels per tick downward */
#define SI_MAX_ALIEN_BULLETS    3

/* Movement */
#define SI_ALIEN_STEP_X         4     /* pixels per horizontal step */
#define SI_ALIEN_DROP_Y         12    /* pixels to drop when reversing */
#define SI_BASE_MOVE_TICKS      30    /* ticks between moves at full grid (600ms) */
#define SI_MIN_MOVE_TICKS       4     /* fastest speed (80ms per step) */
#define SI_SPEED_FACTOR         1     /* ticks reduction per alien killed: (30-4)/31 ~= 0.84, use 1 is close enough */

/* Firing */
#define SI_FIRE_INTERVAL_TICKS  40    /* alien fires every ~800ms base */
#define SI_FIRE_INTERVAL_MIN    15    /* fastest fire rate (~300ms) */

/* Scoring */
#define SI_SCORE_ROW0           40    /* top row (hardest) */
#define SI_SCORE_ROW1           30
#define SI_SCORE_ROW2           20
#define SI_SCORE_ROW3           10    /* bottom row (easiest) */

/* Game rules */
#define SI_MAX_LIVES            3
#define SI_INVASION_Y           (SI_SHIP_Y - SI_ALIEN_HEIGHT)  /* if aliens reach here, game over */
```

---

### Complete State Struct

```c
typedef struct {
    /* LVGL objects -- all pre-allocated */
    lv_obj_t *screen;
    lv_obj_t *aliens[SI_ALIEN_COUNT];
    lv_obj_t *ship;
    lv_obj_t *player_bullet;
    lv_obj_t *alien_bullets[SI_MAX_ALIEN_BULLETS];
    lv_obj_t *score_label;
    lv_obj_t *lives_label;
    lv_obj_t *message_label;

    lv_timer_t *game_timer;

    /* Game state */
    game_state_t state;
    uint16_t score;
    uint8_t  lives;
    uint8_t  aliens_alive;

    /* Alien grid state */
    bool     alien_alive[SI_ALIEN_COUNT];
    int16_t  grid_x;        /* X offset of entire grid (top-left of grid bounding box) */
    int16_t  grid_y;        /* Y offset of entire grid */
    int8_t   grid_dir;      /* +1 = moving right, -1 = moving left */
    uint16_t move_tick_counter;  /* counts up; when >= move_interval, step aliens */
    uint16_t move_interval;      /* ticks between alien steps, decreases as aliens die */

    /* Player ship */
    int16_t  ship_x;

    /* Player bullet */
    int16_t  pbullet_x;
    int16_t  pbullet_y;
    bool     pbullet_active;

    /* Alien bullets */
    int16_t  abullet_x[SI_MAX_ALIEN_BULLETS];
    int16_t  abullet_y[SI_MAX_ALIEN_BULLETS];
    bool     abullet_active[SI_MAX_ALIEN_BULLETS];

    /* Alien fire timing */
    uint16_t fire_tick_counter;
    uint16_t fire_interval;

    /* Simple PRNG state (xorshift16 for deterministic random) */
    uint16_t rng_state;

} space_invaders_state_t;
```

**Struct size estimate:** ~200 bytes (pointers: 42x4=168, int16 fields: ~30 bytes, bools: ~40 bytes, padding). Negligible.

---

### Movement Algorithm for Alien Grid

The alien grid is tracked as a single logical block with an (grid_x, grid_y) offset. Individual alien positions are computed as:

```
alien_screen_x = grid_x + col * (SI_ALIEN_WIDTH + SI_ALIEN_GAP_H)
alien_screen_y = grid_y + row * (SI_ALIEN_HEIGHT + SI_ALIEN_GAP_V)
```

**Step logic (called when `move_tick_counter >= move_interval`):**

1. Compute the leftmost and rightmost living alien columns to find effective grid bounds.
2. Trial-move: `new_grid_x = grid_x + grid_dir * SI_ALIEN_STEP_X`
3. Check if the rightmost living alien in the new position exceeds `SCREEN_WIDTH - SI_ALIEN_WIDTH` or leftmost goes below 0.
4. If hitting an edge:
   - Reverse `grid_dir`
   - Drop: `grid_y += SI_ALIEN_DROP_Y`
   - Do NOT step horizontally this tick (authentic behavior)
5. If not hitting edge:
   - `grid_x = new_grid_x`
6. Update all living alien LVGL object positions.
7. Check if any alien row has reached `SI_INVASION_Y` -- if so, game over.

**Speed scaling:**
```c
move_interval = SI_BASE_MOVE_TICKS - (SI_ALIEN_COUNT - aliens_alive) * SI_SPEED_FACTOR;
if (move_interval < SI_MIN_MOVE_TICKS) move_interval = SI_MIN_MOVE_TICKS;
```

---

### Firing Mechanics

**Player firing:**
- On screen tap (LV_EVENT_CLICKED) while `state == GAME_STATE_PLAYING`:
  - If `pbullet_active == false`: spawn bullet at `(ship_x + SI_SHIP_WIDTH/2, SI_SHIP_Y)`
  - Set `pbullet_active = true`, unhide bullet object
- Each tick: if active, `pbullet_y -= SI_PLAYER_BULLET_SPEED`
- If `pbullet_y + SI_BULLET_HEIGHT < 0`: deactivate, hide

**Alien firing:**
- `fire_tick_counter` increments each tick
- When `fire_tick_counter >= fire_interval`:
  - Reset counter
  - Find a random column that has at least one living alien
  - In that column, find the bottom-most living alien (it fires)
  - Find an inactive alien bullet slot; if all 3 active, skip this cycle
  - Spawn bullet at alien's center-bottom position
  - `fire_interval = SI_FIRE_INTERVAL_TICKS - (SI_ALIEN_COUNT - aliens_alive) / 2` (clamped to minimum)

**PRNG (xorshift16 -- no stdlib dependency, deterministic):**
```c
static uint16_t si_rand(space_invaders_state_t *g) {
    g->rng_state ^= g->rng_state << 7;
    g->rng_state ^= g->rng_state >> 9;
    g->rng_state ^= g->rng_state << 8;
    return g->rng_state;
}
```

---

### Collision Detection

**Player bullet vs. aliens (each tick when pbullet_active):**
```c
for each alien i:
    if not alive: continue
    compute alien_x, alien_y from grid offset + row/col
    AABB test: bullet rect vs alien rect
    if hit:
        alien_alive[i] = false
        hide alien object
        deactivate bullet, hide bullet
        aliens_alive--
        score += row_score[row]
        recalculate move_interval
        if aliens_alive == 0: WIN
        break
```

**Alien bullets vs. player (each tick for each active alien bullet):**
```c
for each alien bullet j (0..2):
    if not active: continue
    abullet_y[j] += SI_ALIEN_BULLET_SPEED
    if abullet_y[j] > SCREEN_HEIGHT: deactivate, hide
    AABB test: alien bullet rect vs ship rect
    if hit:
        deactivate bullet, hide
        lives--
        if lives == 0: GAME_OVER
        else: brief invincibility or just continue
```

---

### Full Function List

| Function | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `space_invaders_create(void)` | Public. Allocates screen, all objects, initializes state, creates timer, loads screen. |
| `space_invaders_destroy(void)` | Public. Deletes timer, deletes screen (frees all child objects). |
| `static si_init_state(si_state_t *g)` | Resets all game variables to starting values. |
| `static si_create_aliens(si_state_t *g)` | Creates 32 alien LVGL objects with row-based colors. |
| `static si_create_ship(si_state_t *g)` | Creates player ship object. |
| `static si_create_bullets(si_state_t *g)` | Creates 1 player + 3 alien bullet objects (hidden). |
| `static si_create_ui(si_state_t *g)` | Creates score, lives, message labels. |
| `static si_update_ship(si_state_t *g)` | Reads touchX, updates ship_x (clamped to screen). |
| `static si_update_player_bullet(si_state_t *g)` | Moves player bullet upward, deactivates if off-screen. |
| `static si_update_alien_bullets(si_state_t *g)` | Moves alien bullets downward, deactivates if off-screen. |
| `static si_move_aliens(si_state_t *g)` | Step-based grid movement with edge detection and drop. |
| `static si_alien_fire(si_state_t *g)` | Timer-based random alien selection and bullet spawn. |
| `static si_check_player_bullet_hits(si_state_t *g)` | AABB collision: player bullet vs all living aliens. |
| `static si_check_alien_bullet_hits(si_state_t *g)` | AABB collision: alien bullets vs player ship. |
| `static si_check_invasion(si_state_t *g)` | Checks if aliens reached ship Y level (instant game over). |
| `static si_update_positions(si_state_t *g)` | Syncs all LVGL object positions from state variables. |
| `static si_update_score_display(si_state_t *g)` | Updates score label text. |
| `static si_update_lives_display(si_state_t *g)` | Updates lives label text. |
| `static si_show_message(si_state_t *g, const char *msg)` | Shows center message, unhides label. |
| `static si_hide_message(si_state_t *g)` | Hides center message label. |
| `static si_recalc_speed(si_state_t *g)` | Recalculates move_interval and fire_interval after kill. |
| `static si_find_grid_bounds(si_state_t *g, int16_t *left_col, int16_t *right_col)` | Finds leftmost/rightmost living columns for edge detection. |
| `static si_rand(si_state_t *g)` | 16-bit xorshift PRNG. |
| `static si_game_timer_cb(lv_timer_t *timer)` | Main game loop: dispatches updates based on state. |
| `static si_screen_click_cb(lv_event_t *e)` | Click handler: fire bullet or transition states. |

---

### Game Timer Callback Structure

```c
static void si_game_timer_cb(lv_timer_t *timer) {
    space_invaders_state_t *g = &g_si;
    (void)timer;

    if (g->state != GAME_STATE_PLAYING) return;

    si_update_ship(g);
    si_update_player_bullet(g);
    si_update_alien_bullets(g);

    /* Alien movement on interval */
    g->move_tick_counter++;
    if (g->move_tick_counter >= g->move_interval) {
        g->move_tick_counter = 0;
        si_move_aliens(g);
    }

    /* Alien firing on interval */
    g->fire_tick_counter++;
    if (g->fire_tick_counter >= g->fire_interval) {
        g->fire_tick_counter = 0;
        si_alien_fire(g);
    }

    /* Collision detection */
    si_check_player_bullet_hits(g);
    si_check_alien_bullet_hits(g);
    si_check_invasion(g);

    /* Update LVGL object positions */
    si_update_positions(g);
}
```

---

### Click Callback State Machine

```c
static void si_screen_click_cb(lv_event_t *e) {
    space_invaders_state_t *g = &g_si;
    (void)e;

    switch (g->state) {
        case GAME_STATE_IDLE:
            /* Start game */
            g->state = GAME_STATE_PLAYING;
            si_hide_message(g);
            break;
        case GAME_STATE_PLAYING:
            /* Fire bullet */
            if (!g->pbullet_active) {
                g->pbullet_x = g->ship_x + SI_SHIP_WIDTH / 2 - SI_BULLET_WIDTH / 2;
                g->pbullet_y = SI_SHIP_Y - SI_BULLET_HEIGHT;
                g->pbullet_active = true;
                lv_obj_clear_flag(g->player_bullet, LV_OBJ_FLAG_HIDDEN);
            }
            break;
        case GAME_STATE_GAME_OVER:
        case GAME_STATE_WIN:
            /* Return to splash */
            space_invaders_destroy();
            splash_screen_create();
            break;
    }
}
```

### Memory Budget Summary

| Item | Bytes |
|------|-------|
| 32 alien `lv_obj_t` (plain obj, minimal style) | ~7,680 (240 each) |
| 1 ship `lv_obj_t` | ~240 |
| 4 bullet `lv_obj_t` | ~960 |
| 3 label `lv_obj_t` (with text buffers) | ~1,050 |
| 1 screen `lv_obj_t` | ~300 |
| Timer allocation | ~64 |
| Static struct (BSS, not heap) | ~210 |
| Misc LVGL internal (child arrays, style cache) | ~1,500 |
| **Total LVGL heap** | **~11,800** |

This is within the same ~12KB budget as Breakout. The static state struct lives in BSS (not LVGL heap), so it costs 0 heap bytes.
